agent-cursor.summary: A developer editor where AI writes the code for you
agent-cursor.description: A VS Code-based AI code editor that understands your entire project's file context and writes or refactors code from plain-language instructions alone. It offers a free plan and a paid plan ($20/mo), with a choice of models including Claude and GPT-4o. Just describe a complex feature or bug fix in natural language and it handles the rest, cutting coding time by at least 2-3x.
agent-cursor.strength: Autonomous coding and best-in-class context understanding
pros: Understands entire files and writes code automatically
Refactors and fixes bugs from natural-language prompts
Free plan plus a choice of multiple AI models
cons: Advanced features require a paid plan ($20/mo)
Responses can lag on large projects
agent-cursor.userReview: Once you use it, there's no going back. My coding speed at least tripled.

agent-devin.summary: The world's first fully autonomous AI software engineer
agent-devin.description: A fully autonomous AI agent from Cognition that takes a GitHub issue and handles writing code, testing, and submitting the PR entirely on its own. It comes with its own browser, shell, and editor, letting it set up an environment and solve problems just like a real developer would, at a premium price of $500+ per month. It drew major attention as the first agent to approach human-developer-level performance on the SWE-bench coding benchmark.
agent-devin.strength: End-to-end autonomous development, from bug fixes to deployment
pros: Automates the entire development process
Resolves GitHub issues automatically
Can develop around the clock
cons: Expensive at $500+ per month
Still struggles with complex business logic
agent-devin.userReview: I filed a PR, went to sleep, and woke up to it ready to merge. Feels like it's from the future.

agent-manus.summary: A general-purpose AI agent that handles web browsing, file work, and coding all on its own
agent-manus.description: A multitasking AI agent that operates a real browser while simultaneously handling research, file creation, and code execution. It runs multiple sub-agents in parallel to complete complex, multi-step tasks autonomously, delivering everything from research reports to draft slide decks in one stop. It currently operates on an invite basis and is one of the most talked-about services in the global AI agent market.
agent-manus.strength: Multitasking across browser control, file handling, and code execution
pros: Fully autonomous multitasking
Performs tasks like an actual computer user
Automates complex research
cons: Currently invite-only with a waitlist
Costs rise for longer tasks
agent-manus.userReview: I asked it to write a research report and it even tracked down the sources for me.

agent-n8n.summary: Connects ChatGPT, Slack, and Notion with no code. Great for automation beginners
agent-n8n.description: An open-source workflow automation platform offering 400+ app connectors and AI nodes. Self-host it and you get unlimited free use, with AI tasks like LLM calls and image generation easy to plug straight into your workflows. It's rapidly spreading among tech startups and developers as a powerful alternative to Zapier.
agent-n8n.userReview: I wired up ChatGPT + Slack + Notion in 30 minutes.

agent-langchain.summary: The go-to library for building AI agents. Past 100 million downloads
agent-langchain.description: An open-source framework for building LLM-powered AI apps and agents, providing core components like RAG, agents, and chains. It supports both Python and JavaScript and connects to nearly every LLM provider, including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google. Most production AI pipelines at AI startups and enterprises alike are built on top of LangChain.
agent-langchain.userReview: Indispensable when you're building a RAG pipeline.

agent-autogpt.summary: Give it a goal and it searches, codes, and executes on its own. Still maturing but full of potential
agent-autogpt.description: An early autonomous AI agent project built on GPT-4 that breaks down and executes tasks on its own once given a goal. It autonomously uses tools like internet search, file saving, and code execution, and it's open-sourced on GitHub. It was the project that first popularized the concept of a fully autonomous AI agent, and development is still going strong.
pros: Fully open source and free to use
A pioneer of the autonomous agent concept
Extensible with custom plugins
cons: True autonomy is still shaky in practice
Complex setup that's tough for beginners
agent-autogpt.userReview: Full autonomy still has a ways to go, but it's plenty capable for automating repetitive tasks.

agent-perplexity-agent.summary: Real-time web data plus automated deep research. Cuts research time by 80%
agent-perplexity-agent.description: Perplexity's deep research feature automatically crawls dozens of web pages on a complex topic and compiles a comprehensive, source-cited report. It can finish tasks like market research, competitor analysis, or literature reviews in just minutes. Subscribing to the Pro plan ($20/mo) unlocks far more deep research runs and more precise analysis.
agent-perplexity-agent.userReview: Way more useful than ChatGPT for competitor analysis and market research.

agent-claude-code.summary: Anthropic's coding agent that runs right in your terminal
agent-claude-code.description: A CLI-based AI agent that reads your file system and edits code directly from the terminal. It leverages a massive context window to grasp an entire codebase and handle complex refactoring, bug fixes, and new features. It's billed based on Claude API usage and integrates with major IDEs like VS Code and JetBrains.
agent-claude-code.userReview: It holds context so well through complex refactors that I can just trust it with the work.

agent-genspark.summary: An all-in-one AI agent that handles search, research, slides, and video in one go
agent-genspark.description: Rather than just listing search results, the AI synthesizes the content directly into a custom 'Spark Page.' Multiple agents work in parallel to produce slides, spreadsheets, images, and even video, completing complex reports from a single prompt. Generous daily free usage lets even non-developers experience the power of AI agents right away.
pros: Completes research and slide decks from a single prompt
Parallel processing across multiple agents
Generous free usage
cons: Still in beta with some rough edges
Costs rise for more complex tasks
agent-genspark.userReview: From research to a finished slide deck in a single prompt. Cut my work time in half.

agent-openai-operator.summary: Drives a real web browser to handle bookings, shopping, and form-filling for you
agent-openai-operator.description: A computer-use AI from OpenAI that operates a real web browser with mouse and keyboard input to carry out online tasks on your behalf. It specializes in automating repetitive web chores like booking flights, filling out forms, and sending emails. It's available to ChatGPT Plus and Pro subscribers, launched first in the US, and is gradually expanding globally.
pros: Automates web tasks by controlling a real browser
No extra cost on top of ChatGPT Plus
Handles repetitive tasks like bookings and shopping automatically
cons: US-first launch with limited access in Korea
Concerns over exposing sensitive account information
agent-openai-operator.userReview: I just told it to book a flight and it actually did the whole thing. Genuinely gave me chills.

agent-zapier-ai.summary: Connects 7,000+ apps and lets AI build automation logic from plain language
agent-zapier-ai.description: Describe an automation workflow in plain language and the AI builds out the logic for you, across integrations with 7,000+ apps. The free plan includes up to 100 tasks per month, letting you build complex automations without a dedicated developer. The AI Zap Builder feature generates a full workflow from a single line of natural language, drastically cutting setup time.
agent-zapier-ai.userReview: Built a Gmail-to-Slack-to-Notion automation in 5 minutes without knowing any code.

llm-chatgpt.summary: Answers anything well. The obvious starting point if you're new to AI
llm-chatgpt.description: The world's most-used AI chatbot, built by OpenAI, offering integrated text, image, and voice handling powered by the GPT-4o model. It's split between a free plan (GPT-4o mini) and a Plus plan ($20/mo, unlimited GPT-4o), with the GPT Store offering additional specialized bots. Thanks to its plugin ecosystem and API accessibility, it has become the AI standard used by more than 100 million people worldwide every week.
pros: 100M+ users worldwide, the strongest ecosystem around
Solid GPT-4o access even on the free plan
Integrated handling of images, voice, and text
cons: Free plan has a daily usage cap
Privacy concerns over data being used for training

llm-claude.summary: Uncannily good at tracking long context. A real powerhouse for writing reports
llm-claude.description: Built by Anthropic, Claude supports a massive context window of up to 200,000 tokens, letting it analyze a whole book's worth of text in one pass. Its writing, coding, and analysis abilities rival GPT-4o, and it particularly excels at processing long documents without losing the thread. It offers a free plan and a Pro plan ($20/mo), and is built with a constitutional AI design aimed at being safe and harmless.
pros: Massive 200K-token context window
Best-in-class for analyzing long documents and reports
Constitutional AI design centered on safety
cons: Free plan has usage limits
Limited real-time web search capability

llm-gemini.summary: Summarizes Google Drive docs instantly. Recommended if you live in the Google ecosystem
llm-gemini.description: A multimodal AI from Google DeepMind that's deeply integrated with Google Workspace, including Docs, Sheets, Gmail, and YouTube. Gemini 2.0 Flash delivers strong speed and cost efficiency, while Google One AI Premium ($19.99/mo) unlocks the most powerful model. It supports 40+ languages, including Korean, and is built into Google devices across Android and Chrome.
pros: Deep integration with Google Workspace (Drive, Gmail)
1M-token context window (1.5 Pro)
Multilingual support including Korean
cons: Less useful outside the Google ecosystem
Top-tier model requires a paid subscription

llm-perplexity.summary: A great substitute for Google search. Trustworthy since every answer includes source links
llm-perplexity.description: An AI search engine that combines real-time internet search with AI-generated answers, displaying source URLs alongside every response. You can choose from multiple models, including GPT-4, Claude, and Mistral, and its deep research feature digs into complex topics in depth. The free version is capped at 5 Pro searches per day, while the Pro plan costs $20/mo.
pros: Every answer comes with source URLs
Real-time web search keeps information current
Deep research enables in-depth analysis
cons: Free plan capped at 5 Pro searches per day
Geared more toward search and research than long-form creative work

llm-copilot.summary: AI built into Word and Excel makes editing genuinely effortless
llm-copilot.description: An AI assistant integrated into Microsoft 365 that helps draft Word documents, analyze Excel data, summarize Teams meetings, and write Outlook emails. It runs on GPT-4 and connects to Bing search to bring in up-to-date information. Microsoft 365 Business subscribers can add it for $30/mo, making it the most natural way for Office users to bring AI into their workflow.

llm-grok.summary: Pulls in real-time info from X (Twitter). Blunt, uncensored answers
llm-grok.description: Built by Elon Musk's xAI, Grok's biggest differentiator is its access to real-time post data from the X (Twitter) platform. The Grok 3 model performs close to GPT-4o on major benchmarks, and it aims for a more humorous, blunt answer style than competing AIs. It's included with an X Premium+ subscription ($16/mo) and is also available via a standalone app and website.
pros: Access to real-time X (Twitter) data
Blunt, humorous answer style
Included with an X Premium subscription
cons: Requires a paid X Premium subscription
Concerns about biased training data

llm-llama.summary: A free AI you run locally. Use it without worrying about personal data leaks
llm-llama.description: An open-source large language model from Meta that you can install on your own computer and run for free, without limits, and without an internet connection. The Llama 3.3 70B version performs on par with GPT-4, and tools like Ollama make it easy to run even on an everyday consumer PC. Since no data ever leaves your machine, it's especially well-suited for handling sensitive internal company work.

llm-mistral.summary: European open-source AI. Fast and lightweight, great for cutting server costs
llm-mistral.description: A highly efficient open-source model from French startup Mistral AI, whose Mixtral MoE (mixture-of-experts) architecture delivers GPT-4-level performance with far less compute. Companies that want a European model compliant with the EU AI Act favor it, and its API costs are significantly cheaper than GPT-4's. The Mistral Large model offers quality solid enough for commercial-grade services.

llm-deepseek.summary: GPT-4-level performance for next to nothing. There's a reason it's hot in developer circles
llm-deepseek.description: An open-source model from the Chinese AI lab DeepSeek, whose R1 reasoning model shook the industry by performing close to GPT-o1. Thanks to a dramatically more efficient training design, its API costs run at a fraction of GPT-4's, sometimes tens of times cheaper. It's open-sourced and can be self-hosted, but because it comes from a Chinese company, caution is warranted when using it for sensitive business data.

llm-notebooklm.summary: Upload a PDF and it answers based only on that document. Best for analyzing internal materials
llm-notebooklm.description: An AI research assistant developed by Google that draws exclusively from the documents, PDFs, and links you upload, effectively eliminating hallucinated information. You can upload multiple documents at once for cross-analysis, and the 'Audio Overview' feature, which turns your content into a podcast-style discussion, has become especially popular. It's currently free to use and has become an essential tool for researchers, students, and business professionals alike.

llm-cohere.summary: Specialized in enterprise document search integration. Handy for building B2B services
llm-cohere.description: An enterprise-grade LLM built for RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) and multi-hop reasoning, designed to combine with internal company documents to produce accurate answers. It supports more than 10 business languages and is favored by finance and legal firms for its strong data security. API costs are lower than GPT-4, making it a cost-effective choice for building large-scale B2B services.

llm-solar.summary: Built by Korean startup Upstage. Remarkably strong performance for its small size
llm-solar.description: Developed by Korean AI startup Upstage, the Solar Pro model has just 22B parameters yet ranks near the top of the OpenLLM leaderboard. It excels at understanding Korean and also offers enterprise document-processing solutions (OCR, layout analysis). On-premise deployment compliant with local regulations makes it especially well-suited for Korean companies where data security is a priority.

llm-hyperclova.summary: Unmatched at understanding Korean context. Worth considering for domestic service development
llm-hyperclova.description: A massive language model trained by Naver primarily on Korean-language data, and widely regarded as outperforming foreign models at understanding Korean culture, idioms, and context. It's available for commercial use via the CLOVA Studio API and is already deployed across Naver's own services, including Search, Shopping, and Clova Note. Support for local regulatory compliance and on-premise deployment makes it popular with finance and healthcare companies.

llm-qwen.summary: Alibaba's open-source model. GPT-4-level performance, free to use
llm-qwen.description: A family of large language models developed by Alibaba Cloud, with the 72B-parameter version of Qwen2.5 released as open source. It ranks at the top among open-source models of its class for coding, math, and reasoning ability, and performs strongly in both Chinese and English. It's free to download on Hugging Face, and a multimodal Qwen-VL version is also available.

llm-yi.description: An open-source model developed by 01.AI, the company founded by former Google Brain leader Kai-Fu Lee. At 34B parameters, it runs comfortably on consumer-grade GPUs and matches or beats Llama 2 70B on major benchmarks like MMLU. It handles multiple languages, including Korean, and is offered under a license that permits commercial use.

llm-huggingchat.summary: Dozens of open-source models in one place. A go-to for comparing models
llm-huggingchat.description: A free AI chatbot run by Hugging Face that lets you switch between a variety of open-source models — Llama 3, Mistral, Falcon, and more — all within a single interface. It's completely free and works right in your browser with no installation, making it an ideal starting point for anyone new to AI models. Researchers and developers also use it heavily to quickly compare and test newly released models.

llm-poe-plus.summary: Compare GPT, Claude, and Gemini side by side. It's fascinating to see how each model's answer style differs
llm-poe-plus.description: A multi-AI platform run by Quora that gives you access to dozens of AI models — ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and more — under a single subscription. It also includes a bot builder that lets developers and businesses create their own AI bots and publish them on the Poe marketplace. For $19.99/mo, you can compare multiple premium AIs cost-effectively, making it popular with AI power users.

img-midjourney.summary: The undisputed leader in artistic image quality. The top pick among professional designers
img-midjourney.description: A service that generates stunning, art-quality images from text prompts inside a Discord channel. The V6 model delivers top-tier results in both photorealistic renderings and fantastical artwork, making it the go-to choice for professional designers and illustrators. Subscriptions run from $10/mo (basic) to $120/mo (heavy usage), and commercial use of generated images is permitted.
pros: Industry-best artistic image quality
Proven quality trusted by professional designers
Commercial use of images is permitted
cons: Discord-based interface creates a barrier to entry
No free plan; starts at $10/mo

img-stablediffusion.summary: Install it on your own PC for unlimited generation. Swap models to change art styles however you like
img-stablediffusion.description: An open-source image generation model released by Stability AI that you can install on your own PC to create unlimited images with no internet connection and no cost. Thousands of free fine-tuned models on Civitai let you apply almost any art style you want, and pairing it with interfaces like AUTOMATIC1111 or ComfyUI lets you fully customize your workflow. Used well, it offers even more creative freedom than Midjourney.
pros: Unlimited free generation via local installation
Swap between thousands of custom models
Fully open source with unmatched creative freedom
cons: Initial installation and setup can be complex
Generation is slow without a high-performance GPU

img-dalle3.summary: Ready to use the moment you subscribe to ChatGPT Plus. No complicated setup required
img-dalle3.description: An image generation model from OpenAI, integrated into ChatGPT Plus ($20/mo), that lets you create and edit images through natural conversation. Text rendering within images has improved significantly, and the model is trained with safeguards designed to minimize copyright issues. Developers can also integrate it into their own services easily via the API.
pros: Included with ChatGPT Plus, no separate subscription needed
Highly accurate text rendering within images
Trained with copyright-safe safeguards
cons: Lower artistic quality compared to Midjourney
Generation speed is somewhat slow

img-flux.summary: Cheaper and faster than Midjourney. Solid enough for commercial projects too
img-flux.description: A next-generation image generation model from Black Forest Labs, the original team behind Stable Diffusion, with the Flux.1 Dev version released as open source. It rivals Midjourney in photorealistic portrayal of people and text rendering, and a version that runs free on your own GPU is also available. Fast generation speed and consistently high quality make it reliable enough for commercial projects.

img-ideogram.summary: Renders text in images flawlessly. Highly recommended for thumbnails and logos
img-ideogram.description: A service that far outperforms other image generation AIs at accurately placing text inside images. It's specialized for text-heavy design work like posters, banners, YouTube thumbnails, and logos, and its Magic Prompt feature automatically expands simple descriptions into rich, detailed prompts. Even the free plan allows 10-25 images per day, with paid plans starting at $8/mo.
pros: The industry's strongest text rendering within images
10-25 images per day even on the free plan
Specialized for thumbnail and poster design
cons: Average image quality for non-text visuals
Limited when it comes to complex compositions

img-leonardo.summary: Generous free credits. Start generating right on the web, no login required
img-leonardo.description: An AI image generation platform specialized for game assets and content creation, offering 150 free tokens every day. A wide range of fine-tuned models let you achieve virtually any style you want, from photorealistic photos to anime and game art. It also supports real-time image generation, Canvas editing, and AI video generation, making it an all-in-one platform optimized for designers and game developers.

img-firefly.summary: Free to use for Adobe subscribers, no extra cost. Copyright-safe
img-firefly.description: An image generation AI that Adobe trained exclusively on commercially safe data, guaranteeing the commercial copyright of generated images. It's fully integrated across Adobe's app lineup, including Photoshop's 'Generative Fill' and Illustrator's 'Text Effects.' Creative Cloud subscriptions include 25 credits per month, with additional credits available for purchase on the web version.
pros: 100% guaranteed commercial copyright safety
Seamless integration with Photoshop and Illustrator
Included with a Creative Cloud subscription
cons: Non-Adobe CC subscribers must purchase credits separately
Generation quality is lower than Midjourney's

img-krea.summary: Turns rough sketches into polished visuals in real time. Ideal for visualizing ideas
img-krea.description: Built around real-time AI image rendering, it transforms your sketches into high-quality images the instant you draw them. It's optimized for quickly visualizing concept art and product design ideas, and also offers an AI upscaler and image enhancement features. The free version lets you try most of the core features, making it a powerful tool for the early idea stage.

img-civitai.summary: Download thousands of custom models for free. Any art style you want, it's here
img-civitai.description: The largest model hub in the Stable Diffusion community, where users freely share tens of thousands of self-made models (checkpoints, LoRAs, embeddings). Specialized models exist for nearly every style imaginable — photorealistic people, anime, architecture, product photography, and more — and you can either generate images directly through the web UI or download model files to run locally. It serves as the central hub of the AI image generation community.

img-runway.summary: Both image and video generation in one place. An all-purpose tool for creative work
img-runway.description: A creative AI tool that offers both image generation and AI video generation on a single platform. It accepts text, image, and video inputs to produce high-quality output, and is used by advertising agencies and film studios alike. Basic image generation is available on the free plan, with paid plans starting at $15/mo.

img-playground.summary: 1,000 free images a day. Midjourney-level quality makes it the best value around
img-playground.description: It gained popularity thanks to a generous free plan that allows up to 500 images per day. The Playground v2 model delivers artistic quality close to Midjourney's, and its intuitive UI makes it easy for beginners to pick up. A community feed lets you browse other users' prompts and results, helping you improve quickly.

img-bluewillow.summary: Free to use on Discord. Quality that rivals Midjourney
img-bluewillow.description: A free image generation service that operates as a Discord bot, built on Stable Diffusion with its own fine-tuning to deliver solid quality. Like Midjourney, it has a community Discord where you can browse and learn from other users' creations. Thanks to its completely free model, it's an easy recommendation for anyone just getting started with AI image generation.

img-tensor.summary: Run SD models in your browser, no install needed. Great for fast prototyping
img-tensor.description: A cloud AI platform that lets you run a wide range of Stable Diffusion-based models directly in your browser, no local GPU required. It offers custom model training (fine-tuning) and community model sharing, playing a role similar to Civitai. Free credits are included, making it useful for individual creators and developers who need to prototype quickly.

img-dreamstudio.summary: Stability AI's official service. Get instant API access to the latest SD models
img-dreamstudio.description: The official image generation service run by Stability AI, giving instant access to the latest Stable Diffusion models through both an API and a web interface, with no installation required. Developers can grab an API key and integrate image generation into their app with just a few lines of code. Its pay-as-you-go credit system scales flexibly from small side projects to large production services.

img-imagefx.summary: Image generation inside the Google ecosystem, with smooth Gemini integration
img-imagefx.description: An image generation service built on Google's Imagen 3 model, delivering standout performance for realistic, highly detailed images. You can start for free with just a Google account, and it integrates tightly with Gemini so you can generate and edit images through natural conversation. Commercial use requires checking the license terms separately.

img-bing.summary: Free with just a Microsoft account, and DALL-E 3-powered quality
img-bing.description: A free image generation service anyone can use by signing in with a Microsoft account, powered by OpenAI's DALL-E 3 model. It's integrated with Bing Search, so you can access it directly at bing.com/create with no separate app needed. There's a daily generation limit, but it's a great way for beginners to experience DALL-E 3 quality at no cost.

img-picfinder.summary: Generate endless image variations with one click, perfect for quick idea sketches
img-picfinder.description: A service built for rapid idea exploration, letting you keep generating and remixing similar images with a single click. Even without a complex prompt, simple keywords are enough to produce a continuous stream of images in different styles. It's free to use and handy for nailing down a visual direction early in planning or design.

img-stockimg.summary: Built for commercial design work. Logos, posters, and book covers done in minutes
img-stockimg.description: An AI image generation platform tailored for commercial design work like social media banners, logos, posters, book covers, and packaging. Just pick the design type you want and it instantly produces customized results, giving you professional-grade output with no design expertise required. Commercial usage rights are included with generated images, making it practical for small business owners and marketers.

img-getimg.summary: Generation and editing in one platform, no need to juggle separate workflows
img-getimg.description: Brings text-to-image generation, upscaling, inpainting, outpainting, ControlNet, and more together in a single platform. It supports over 200 AI models, letting you choose between Stable Diffusion, FLUX, or proprietary models. It's also available as an API, making it easy for developers to integrate into their own services.

img-seaart.summary: Stunning photorealistic quality, especially strong at generating people
img-seaart.description: An AI image generation platform recognized as one of the best in the industry for photorealistic portrait quality. It supports a wide range of art-style models and LoRAs, plus consistent character generation (IP-Adapter) and video generation features. Free daily credits are provided, and it particularly excels at social media content and profile picture creation.

edit-magnific.summary: Restores blurry photos to 4K. Feels like magic for reviving old pictures
edit-magnific.description: Goes beyond simple upscaling with a 'super-resolution' technique where AI redraws fine textures, restoring blurry photos to 4K-plus quality. It's widely considered the industry leader in image restoration, and is heavily used by professional photographers and Midjourney users to polish their final output. It's a paid service at $39.99/mo, but satisfaction with output quality is very high.

edit-canva.summary: The design essential. AI background removal and Magic Eraser make editing effortless
edit-canva.description: An online design platform used by 100 million people worldwide, packed with AI editing features like background removal, Magic Erase, and generative fill. Combining tens of thousands of templates with AI tools lets you quickly produce social media content, presentations, and marketing materials with no design expertise needed. The free plan is plenty capable on its own, and the Pro plan is $12.99/mo.

edit-photoroom.summary: Removes e-commerce product photo backgrounds in a second. Run your shop without a photographer
edit-photoroom.description: Uses AI to remove backgrounds in a single second and swap in any background you want, turning product photos into professional-grade e-commerce images. It supports both a smartphone app and a web version, with batch processing to edit dozens of photos at once. It's especially popular among social commerce sellers and small online shop owners.

edit-clipdrop.summary: Add lighting, swap backgrounds, retouch faces. An all-in-one photo editing toolkit
edit-clipdrop.description: An image editing API platform run by Stability AI that offers professional editing tools like background removal, inpainting, outpainting, and AI relighting. It's used across a wide range of commercial work — ad photography, product image retouching, portrait editing — and integrates easily into services via its API. You can start with free credits and pay based on usage after that.

edit-topaz.summary: Industry-leading noise reduction. The photographer's go-to right after Lightroom
edit-topaz.description: A professional photo enhancement tool that combines AI-powered noise reduction, motion blur correction, and upscaling, widely regarded as the best in the industry for noise reduction. It dramatically improves the quality of low-light and high-ISO shots, and also works as a Lightroom and Photoshop plugin. It's desktop software priced at $199 (one-time) or a $99/year subscription.

edit-cleanup.summary: Cleanly removes unwanted people or objects from your photos
edit-cleanup.description: Paint over the area you want to remove with a brush and AI fills in the background naturally, cleanly erasing unwanted elements. It works right in your browser with no install needed, and is especially handy for removing passersby from tourist photos or logos from ad banners. Basic features are free, with high-resolution export as a paid option.

edit-pixelcut.summary: Mobile-friendly AI background removal and design editing
edit-pixelcut.description: A mobile-optimized AI image editing app that handles background removal, product photo generation, resizing, and adding text with a single tap. Its automatic product photo generation feature for online sellers is especially powerful, and it can batch-process hundreds of images at once. It consistently ranks near the top of app store charts in the e-commerce photo editing category.

edit-upscayl.summary: A free AI image upscaler you install and run on your own computer
edit-upscayl.description: An open-source AI upscaler you install locally and use for free without an internet connection, with GPU acceleration for fast processing. It supports various AI models like ESRGAN and RealESRGAN, and handles a range of inputs including photos, screenshots, and anime images. Since there's no need to upload files like with cloud services, you can use it without worrying about privacy.

edit-removebg.summary: The original background remover — fast and accurate
edit-removebg.description: The pioneer of AI background removal services, delivering high-precision background removal in just 5 seconds and long established as the industry standard. It handles complex edges like hair cleanly, and integrates easily into your own app via its API. The free version only provides low-resolution output, while high-resolution exports require purchasing credits.

edit-photoshop.summary: Generative Fill conjures up backgrounds that were never there
edit-photoshop.description: The industry-standard image editing software, now integrated with Adobe's generative AI (powered by Firefly). Just type a text description into a selected area and it naturally generates and fills in backgrounds or objects. Generative Expand can even automatically extend the image beyond its original canvas, and AI has made classic tools like the eraser, selection, and retouching far more powerful. Separate Firefly credits apply on top of your CC subscription.

edit-magiceraser.summary: A dead-simple eraser tool that doesn't even require a login
edit-magiceraser.description: An ultra-simple AI eraser service you can use instantly with no sign-up or login — just upload an image, paint over what you want gone, and you're done. Its fast processing speed and clean results make it ideal for quick, simple edits. It's free to use and requires no software installation, making it one of the most accessible erasing tools around.

edit-hama.summary: A free tool that cleanly erases things with a single drag
edit-hama.description: An inpainting service where you brush over unwanted elements in a photo and AI instantly restores the background naturally. It's especially strong at accurately handling larger areas than Cleanup.pictures, and it's free to use on the web. It produces particularly natural results for restoring nature scenes, like removing power lines or people from landscape photos.

edit-watermark.summary: Seamlessly erases watermarks from paid images (use with caution)
edit-watermark.description: A service that uses AI to automatically detect and remove watermarks, logos, and text overlays from images. It can be legally used to erase unwanted timestamps or text overlays from content you created yourself. However, removing watermarks from material copyrighted by others can raise legal issues, so caution is advised.

edit-bigjpg.summary: An upscaler specialized in enlarging anime-style artwork
edit-bigjpg.description: An AI service specialized in upscaling anime, comic, and illustration images, delivering especially natural results on line-art-heavy pictures compared to photorealistic photos. You can choose an enlargement factor from 2x up to 16x, and the web version is free to use. It's a popular choice for restoring old, low-resolution anime screenshots to high definition.

edit-waifu2x.summary: A classic 2x upscaler beloved by anime fans
edit-waifu2x.description: A classic deep-learning tool for anime image upscaling, offered free on the web. It applies 2x enlargement along with noise reduction to sharpen and restore low-resolution anime images. It runs directly in your browser with no heavy software required, and it has been a community favorite for years.

edit-vance.summary: A versatile toolkit for fixing blurry photos, colorizing black-and-white images, and more
edit-vance.description: A collection of AI-powered image enhancement tools offering blurry photo sharpening, noise removal, automatic black-and-white colorization, face restoration, and more. It's especially handy for reviving old family photos or black-and-white pictures, and each feature can be used independently like a standalone tool. Besides a monthly subscription, it also supports pay-per-use pricing for individual features.

edit-photoai.summary: Trains on your photos to generate profile pictures in a variety of styles
edit-photoai.description: Feed the AI several photos of your face and it automatically generates profile pictures in various styles—LinkedIn headshots, fashion editorials, game characters, and more. It lets you get professional-grade profile photos without a studio shoot, making it popular for job hunting and social media. A subscription starting at $29/mo lets you keep generating new photos.

edit-retouch4me.summary: The ultimate portrait retoucher for skin smoothing, blemish removal, and more
edit-retouch4me.description: An AI portrait retouching plugin built for professional photographers, automatically handling detailed fixes like blemish removal, skin tone evening, teeth whitening, and eye sharpening. As a Photoshop/Lightroom plugin, it slots directly into existing editing workflows, making it widely used among photographers. Its results are praised for looking natural rather than overly artificial.

edit-evoto.summary: The AI tool that revolutionized wedding photo retouching
edit-evoto.description: AI retouching software built for wedding, ID, and portrait photographers, with standout batch processing that can retouch hundreds of photos at once. You can finely tune AI retouching intensity by area—skin, makeup, outfit, background—making it easy to achieve natural-looking results. Wedding photographers report it cuts post-production time by more than 70%.

edit-cutout.summary: Does it all, from video background removal to ID photo creation
edit-cutout.description: A one-stop platform for content editing tasks like image and video background removal, ID photo creation, AI avatar generation, and color correction. Its real-time person segmentation in videos stands out in particular, and it's widely used by creators and marketers. Basic features are available on the free plan, while advanced features require purchasing credits.

vid-runway-gen3.summary: The leader in photorealistic video generation, built for filmmaking
vid-runway-gen3.description: An AI that converts text or images into high-resolution video up to 10 seconds long. The Gen-3 Alpha model boasts industry-leading quality in physical motion and lighting. It's actually used in Hollywood films, ads, and music videos, and it also comes with built-in video editing tools. The free plan includes a watermark; the Standard plan starts at $15/mo.
pros: Quality proven by real Hollywood use
Built-in video editing tools
Industry-leading physics and lighting
cons: Free plan always includes a watermark
Can't generate long videos (10-second max)

vid-luma.summary: High-quality video generation that's genuinely usable even for free
vid-luma.description: An AI video generation service from Luma Labs that quickly turns text or image prompts into videos up to 9 seconds long. Natural physical motion and camera movement make it great for rapid concept visualization. The free plan allows a set number of generations each month, and paid plans start at $29.99/mo, keeping the barrier to entry low for creators.
pros: High-quality output even on the free plan
Natural-looking camera motion
Fast generation speed
cons: Limited to 9 seconds max
Costs rise when upgrading to a paid plan

vid-kling.summary: A realistic video AI out of China that burst onto the scene recently
vid-kling.description: An AI video generation service developed by China's Kuaishou, capable of producing high-quality videos up to 30 seconds long—longer than most competing services. It especially excels at realistic human motion and facial expressions, and supports both image-to-video and text-to-video. Generous free credits have made it a strong alternative to Runway and Luma.
pros: Supports the longest generation length at up to 30 seconds
Generous free credits
Excellent human motion and facial expression
cons: Accessibility issues since it's a Chinese service
Poor Korean-language support

vid-pika.summary: Strong at anime-style visuals and smooth motion
vid-pika.description: A service that generates AI video from text, images, or video clips. Starting with Pika 2.0, it added a 'Scene Editing' feature that lets you selectively modify specific elements within a video. It excels at natural character movement and short narrative videos, and its easy-to-use interface makes it accessible even to non-experts. It offers a range of plans, including a free tier.
pros: Scene Editing lets you modify individual elements
Interface that's easy even for non-experts
Free plan available
cons: Shorter generation length compared to other services
Weaker at highly photorealistic video

vid-sora.summary: OpenAI's ambitious model for creating up to 20 seconds of high-quality video from text
vid-sora.description: A text-to-video model developed by OpenAI, known for understanding physical laws and rendering complex scene composition with striking realism. Its demo videos stunned the world upon release, and it's currently being rolled out first to ChatGPT Pro subscribers. It can generate videos up to 1080p and 20 seconds long, and Hollywood studios are already testing it.
pros: Top-tier video quality grounded in an understanding of physical laws
Generates up to 1080p, 20-second videos
OpenAI's brand and credibility
cons: Requires a ChatGPT Pro subscription ($200/mo)
No API available, limiting access

vid-haiper.summary: Short but impactful high-resolution video generation
vid-haiper.description: A service that quickly generates high-resolution videos 2-4 seconds long from a text prompt or image. It generates faster than competing services and renders fine detail well, making it well suited for social media content and ad creative. You can start with free credits, and affordable paid plans keep it accessible for creators.

vid-stablevideo.summary: Open-source and great for turning a single image into video
vid-stablevideo.description: An image-to-video model from Stability AI, specialized in turning still images into naturally moving video. The Stable Video Diffusion model is released as open source, so you can run it yourself or integrate it into other services. Both a cloud version and a local-install version are available, and you can adjust motion speed with a 2-25 frame setting.

vid-vidu.description: A Chinese AI video generation service that specializes in maintaining a character's visual consistency while generating natural movement. It's highly useful for producing distinctively styled animated or advertising videos, and it supports both 2D and 3D. It offers a free plan and paid subscription options, along with a variety of presets for creators.

vid-minimax.summary: Strong at flashy effects and dynamic scenes
vid-minimax.description: An AI video generation service from China's MiniMax, also known as Hailuo AI, that stands out for dynamic, flashy effects. It can generate videos up to 6 seconds long and supports both text and image input. Even the free version produces solidly high-quality video, and its user base is growing fast.

vid-veo.summary: Google's all-out video generation model (coming to YouTube)
vid-veo.description: A high-quality text-to-video model developed by Google DeepMind, known for realistic video generation grounded in an understanding of physical laws. It excels at cinematic camera work and continuous motion, and will be integrated into Google Workspace and YouTube in stages. It's offered to enterprise customers via the Vertex AI API, and is considered OpenAI Sora's strongest rival in the AI video market.

vid-morph.summary: Chains text-to-video clips together like a storyboard
vid-morph.description: An AI video creation platform that lets you link multiple video clips together in a storyboard format to produce a complete short film. You can finely specify style, camera, and narration for each scene, making it well suited for producing videos with a coherent narrative structure. It's a tool favored by digital storytellers and small video production teams.

vid-moonvalley.summary: High-quality cinematic video generation via Discord
vid-moonvalley.description: An AI video generation service run as a Discord bot, specialized in producing cinematic video with film-like lighting and color. Like Midjourney, it offers a community Discord where you can learn by studying other users' results. It operates on an invite basis with a long waitlist, but word has spread thanks to the quality of its output.

vid-genmo.description: An AI service that adds camera movement and depth to still images, producing footage that looks like it was filmed in 3D space. Its 'animate a photo' feature, which brings old photos or paintings to life, is especially popular. Offers a free plan and features an intuitive interface that's easy for AI video beginners to pick up.

vid-deforum.summary: Generates geometric visuals that seem to dance to music
vid-deforum.description: An open-source project built on Stable Diffusion that generates psychedelic AI videos with scenes that morph in time to the beat. It produces uniquely styled output for music videos, art films, and social media content. Published as open source on GitHub, so technically inclined creators can freely customize it.

vid-kaiber.summary: The stylish AI behind Linkin Park's music video
vid-kaiber.description: An AI video platform specialized for music videos and music content, known for being used in the production of real music videos by artists including Linkin Park. Its standout 'music video AI' feature analyzes audio so the visuals evolve in sync with the beat. A subscription plan starting at $8/mo makes it accessible for music creators.

vid-zeroscope.summary: A free, watermark-free open-source text-to-video model
vid-zeroscope.description: A free, open-source text-to-video model available on Hugging Face that generates 576px-resolution videos with no watermark. It can be run directly on your own machine or in Colab with no commercial service required, and also supports custom fine-tuning. Quality lags behind paid services, but it's a genuinely free way to experiment with AI video generation, making it useful for researchers and developers.

vid-modelscope.summary: Short and bizarre, but perfect for making memes
vid-modelscope.description: An early text-to-video model developed by Alibaba's DAMO Academy, freely accessible on Hugging Face. The output tends to be surreal and unstable, but that very quality has made it paradoxically popular for internet memes and art projects. It also holds historical significance as a snapshot of how far text-to-video technology has come.

vid-animatediff.summary: Brings Stable Diffusion art to life with smooth motion
vid-animatediff.description: An open-source module that adds natural motion to still images generated with Stable Diffusion. It integrates with ComfyUI and A1111, smoothly animating characters, backgrounds, particles, and other elements. Since it requires manual installation, the initial setup has a learning curve, but it offers a high degree of creative freedom and is completely free.

vid-hotshot.summary: A lightweight, fast generator built for making GIFs
vid-hotshot.description: An AI service that generates high-quality GIF animations from text prompts, specialized for short looping clips. It's optimized for endlessly looping video content like social media stickers, reaction GIFs, and short ad creative. Built on Stable Diffusion, it boasts fast generation speeds and is available as both a mobile app and a web version.

vid-neverends.summary: Makes the person in your photo dance and act
vid-neverends.description: A service that lets you upload a still photo of a person, then animates them dancing or performing various movements using AI. It produces distinctive results for social media challenges and entertainment content. It's built on deep learning-based pose transfer technology and is easy to use directly in the browser.

vedit-capcut.summary: A must-have for short-form editing, with AI captions and effects
vedit-capcut.description: A video editing app developed by ByteDance (TikTok's parent company) that offers every AI feature short-form editors need, for free — automatic AI captions, background removal, text-to-speech, and effect recommendations. It comes packed with thousands of templates and stickers optimized for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts formats. It's one of the most widely used mobile video editing apps in the world, and a PC version is also available.

vedit-opus.summary: Automatically cuts long YouTube videos into highlight shorts
vedit-opus.description: AI analyzes long YouTube, Zoom, or podcast videos and automatically extracts the segments most likely to go viral, turning them into short-form clips. It handles auto-captioning, reframing, and caption design in a single click, exporting in formats ready to upload straight to TikTok, Reels, or Shorts. The free plan is capped at 60 minutes of processing per month, and the Pro plan starts at $19/mo.

vedit-vrew.summary: A homegrown Korean gem that auto-generates captions from audio
vedit-vrew.description: An AI video editing tool developed by VoyagerX (Korea) that recognizes video audio with over 99% accuracy to auto-generate captions, letting you edit the corresponding video segment just by clicking the text. Its AI auto-cut feature bulk-removes silences and mistakes, and it also supports multilingual caption translation across Korean, English, Japanese, and more. Even the free plan gives you plenty of core features, making it the most widely used editing tool among Korean YouTubers.

vedit-descript.summary: Edits video like a word processor edits text
vedit-descript.description: Converts video or podcasts into a text transcript, then offers an innovative editing workflow where deleting text automatically deletes the corresponding video segment, just like editing a document. Its AI Overdub feature clones your voice, letting you fix a misspoken line just by typing the correction — no re-recording needed. The Creator plan starts at $24/mo, and it's especially useful for podcasters, interviewers, and educational video creators.

vedit-invideo.summary: Give it just text and get a full video with captions and voiceover
vedit-invideo.description: Enter a text prompt or article URL, and AI automatically assembles a script, stock footage, captions, and AI voiceover into a finished video. It comes with over 5,000 templates and a library of 16 million stock media assets, specialized for producing marketing videos for social media. The Business plan starts at $30/mo and includes team collaboration features.

vedit-premiere.summary: Adobe's AI features cut editing time (built for professionals)
vedit-premiere.description: Adobe's professional video editing software, which uses AI-powered 'Sensei' technology for automatic reframing, audio noise removal, color matching, and scene edit detection. It integrates seamlessly with the rest of the Adobe ecosystem — After Effects, Audition, Photoshop — and since 2024 its Firefly-powered 'Generative Extend' feature can naturally extend a video clip using AI. Included in a Creative Cloud subscription at $55/mo (or $23/mo for a single app).

vedit-munch.summary: Analyzes trends to turn your most viral-worthy moments into shorts
vedit-munch.description: GPT-4-powered AI analyzes long-form video and automatically extracts the segments most likely to go viral, weighing engagement, trends, and keyword relevance. It adds auto-captions, logos, and B-roll to the extracted clips and automatically reformats them to the optimal resolution and aspect ratio for each platform. The Pro plan starts at $49/mo and supports multi-channel distribution across LinkedIn, YouTube, Twitter, and more.

vedit-filmora.summary: Beginner-friendly, with a wide range of AI editing effects
vedit-filmora.description: A beginner-friendly video editing tool from Wondershare packed with around 30 AI features, including background removal, noise removal, motion tracking, speech-to-text, and automatic highlight generation. Its intuitive drag-and-drop interface comes with thousands of effects, transitions, and title templates. Available from an annual plan at $49.99/year, supporting both Windows and Mac.

vedit-kapwing.summary: A collaborative video editor that runs right in your browser
vedit-kapwing.description: An online video editing platform that runs directly in the browser with no installation, offering AI-powered auto-captioning, background removal, silence removal, and resizing. Like Google Docs, it supports real-time collaboration with teammates, and its templates make it quick to produce social media content. The free plan adds a watermark, while the Pro plan starts at $16/mo.

vedit-autocut.summary: Automatically deletes silent gaps in your footage
vedit-autocut.description: An editing automation tool that uses AI to automatically detect and remove silences, hesitations, and flubbed lines from video. It also works as a plugin for Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, and Final Cut Pro, cutting editing time by more than 70% on average. It's considered an essential tool for creators who frequently edit long-form content, like YouTubers, instructors, and podcasters, and is available starting at $9/mo.

vedit-wisecut.summary: Turns long videos into shorts and auto-adds matching music
vedit-wisecut.description: An all-in-one editing tool where AI analyzes a long video, automatically cuts it into short-form clips, and even adds background music that matches the mood. It also includes auto-captioning, punch-in cuts, and voice-based chapter splitting, plus automatic silence removal. Offered in tiers from a free plan (1 hour of video per month) up to a Pro plan at $47/mo.

vedit-rawshorts.summary: Enter a text script and get an animated video
vedit-rawshorts.description: A platform where AI automatically generates animated infographic videos from a text script or data you provide. Its drag-and-drop editor lets you freely customize characters, icons, and charts, and it's specialized for producing explainer videos for corporate promotion, education, and marketing. The Business plan starts at $149/mo and supports team collaboration and brand kits.

vedit-pictory.summary: Paste in a blog post URL and it turns it into a video
vedit-pictory.description: Paste in a blog post URL or a long block of text, and the AI extracts the key points and automatically assembles a video from stock footage, captions, and an AI voiceover. It can also automatically turn long webinars or podcast videos into short social-ready clips. Plans start at $23/mo with the Starter tier, making it ideal for content marketers and bloggers looking to repurpose content into video.

vedit-gliacloud.summary: Automatically turns news articles into video news (a long-standing leader)
vedit-gliacloud.description: A media-focused platform that uses AI to automatically analyze news articles, press releases, and text content, then converts them into video news packages by combining relevant footage, images, and graphics. It's a proven solution used by major global news organizations like Reuters, AP, and Bloomberg, capable of producing videos in minutes without any editor intervention. Runs on custom enterprise-only pricing.

vedit-elai.summary: Turns PowerPoint slides into videos with an AI avatar presenting them
vedit-elai.description: Upload a PowerPoint or Google Slides file and this platform automatically generates a video of an AI avatar presenting your slides. It converts text to speech in 65+ languages, making it easy to produce multilingual training and onboarding videos complete with captions. The Basic plan starts at $29/mo, and custom avatar creation is also supported.

vedit-deepbrain.summary: Broadcast-quality virtual anchor videos (made in Korea)
vedit-deepbrain.description: Developed by the Korean startup DeepBrain AI, this hyper-realistic AI avatar video platform lets you type in text and get an AI news anchor speaking naturally, just like a real person, in minutes. It's an enterprise solution actually used by major Korean broadcasters like KBS and MBC, as well as financial and educational institutions, and supports 80+ AI anchor models and 80+ languages. The Starter plan starts at $30/mo.

vedit-hourone.summary: Just add text and get a polished news report video
vedit-hourone.description: This platform combines AI avatars with text-to-video technology so that simply entering a script produces broadcast-quality presentation, training, or news-style videos automatically. It supports 100+ pre-built AI avatars and 40+ languages, with a particular strength in video localization. The Teams plan starts at $25/mo, and API integration is also available.

vedit-synthesia-edit.summary: Edit videos just by editing text — no video editing skills required
vedit-synthesia-edit.description: This Synthesia editing feature lets you update the script of a video featuring an AI avatar simply by editing the text — the avatar is automatically regenerated to speak the new lines, updating the video instantly. You can revise the content anytime without reshooting or re-editing, making it ideal for training and product demo videos that need regular updates. The Starter plan starts at $22/mo.

vedit-heygen-trans.summary: Translates your video's language while even matching the lip movements
vedit-heygen-trans.description: HeyGen's Video Translation feature uses AI to convert a speaker's voice, intonation, and even lip movements to match the target language, producing naturally multilingual videos. It supports 40+ languages, and because it preserves the original speaker's voice characteristics, the result sounds far more natural than traditional dubbing. Pricing is per-minute, with custom plans available for enterprise customers.

vedit-rask.summary: One tool that handles both video dubbing and translation
vedit-rask.description: An AI localization platform that automatically translates and dubs video content into 130+ languages, preserving the original speaker's vocal tone as closely as possible while also matching lip sync. It handles caption generation, translation, and dubbing in a single workflow, and is widely used by YouTubers, corporate training teams, and content agencies. The Starter plan starts at $60/mo, and team collaboration is also supported.

code-github.summary: One tab, one block of code. Practically every developer uses it
code-github.description: An AI code autocomplete tool co-developed by GitHub and OpenAI that analyzes the context of the code you're writing and completes the next line, function, or entire block with a single Tab press. It works as a plugin for major IDEs like VS Code, JetBrains, and Neovim, and supports 70+ programming languages. Individual plans are $10/mo ($100/year), and business plans are $19/user/mo.
pros: Supports the widest range of IDEs and languages
Excellent context-aware code completion
Seamless integration with the GitHub ecosystem
cons: Requires a paid $10/mo subscription
Security-conscious companies should be cautious about code being transmitted externally

code-replit.summary: Code and deploy right in your browser — build a portfolio site in an hour
code-replit.description: A cloud IDE platform that lets you code, run, and deploy in 50+ languages entirely from your web browser, with nothing to install. The AI Replit Agent can build an app from natural-language instructions alone and instantly deploy it to a public URL. A free plan is available, and the Core plan starts at $25/mo.
pros: One-stop coding, running, and deployment in the browser
Built-in AI Agent that builds apps from natural language
Supports 50+ languages even on the free plan
cons: Performance is limited compared to a local environment
Not well suited for managing large-scale projects

code-supermaven.summary: Faster than GitHub Copilot — you can actually feel the difference in completion speed
code-supermaven.description: An ultra-fast AI code completion tool built separately by the Cursor founding team, boasting response times under 300ms — noticeably faster than Copilot. It leverages a 3-million-token context window to reference an entire large codebase when generating suggestions. It's available as a VS Code plugin, and even the free plan offers unlimited fast completions.
pros: Ultra-fast completions under 300ms
Unlimited usage on the free plan
Supports a massive 3-million-token context window
cons: IDE support is limited, focused mainly on VS Code
Less well known than Copilot

code-codeium.summary: Free, yet Copilot-level. Highly recommended for students and individual developers
code-codeium.description: A free AI code completion and chat plugin from Windsurf (Codeium's parent company), offered entirely free to individual users. It supports 70+ languages and 40+ IDEs, with a key strength in context understanding based on full codebase search. Teams/Enterprise plans are paid, and on-premise deployment is available to ensure code security.
pros: Completely free for individual users
Broad support across 70+ languages and 40+ IDEs
Strong whole-codebase context understanding
cons: Team and enterprise plans require a separate paid subscription
Suggestion accuracy lags somewhat behind Copilot

code-tabnine.summary: Your code never leaves your environment — ideal for companies that need airtight security
code-tabnine.description: A privacy-first AI code completion tool where your code data is never sent to external servers. It supports on-premise deployment, letting you run the model on your own servers or local environment, which makes it a favorite among security-sensitive industries like finance, healthcare, and defense. The Pro plan is $12/user/mo, Enterprise is quote-based, and it supports major IDEs like VS Code and JetBrains.
pros: On-premise deployment keeps code from ever being sent externally
Well suited for security-sensitive industries like finance and healthcare
Plugin support for major IDEs
cons: Free plan features are limited
UI/UX feels relatively dated compared to other tools

code-amazonq.summary: Specialized for writing AWS service code — handy for cloud infrastructure work
code-amazonq.description: An AI coding assistant developed by Amazon, optimized for writing AWS service integration code, CloudFormation, CDK, and Lambda functions. It offers code completion, bug fixes, security vulnerability scanning, and code explanations, and integrates with the AWS console, VS Code, and JetBrains. It's free for individual AWS accounts, with the Pro plan priced at $19/user/mo.

code-jetbrains.summary: If you use IntelliJ and friends, this is the AI that fits best
code-jetbrains.description: An AI coding assistant built directly into JetBrains IDEs (IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, WebStorm, GoLand, and more) that understands each IDE's context better than any third-party tool. AI code completion, error explanations, refactoring, test generation, and documentation features are all available instantly within the IDE. The AI features are included in the JetBrains All Products Pack subscription, and a standalone AI plan is $10/mo.

code-v0.summary: Just describe it, and it builds your website UI instantly
code-v0.description: An AI-powered UI generation tool from Vercel that takes a plain text description and instantly produces fully working web component code built on React and shadcn/ui. You can interactively preview the generated code, edit it, and export it directly into a Next.js project. It's available as a free plan with 200 credits/month, plus a Pro plan at $20/mo.

code-cody.summary: A smart assistant that understands your entire codebase before answering
code-cody.description: An AI coding assistant from Sourcegraph that searches and references not just local files but your entire codebase, documentation, and open-source repositories to deliver more accurate answers. You can choose from multiple LLM backends, including Claude, GPT-4, and Gemini, and it's available as a plugin for VS Code and JetBrains. It's free for individuals, and the Enterprise plan supports on-premise deployment.

code-blackbox.summary: The AI that skips the copy-pasting for your coding questions
code-blackbox.description: An AI coding tool that combines code autocomplete, AI code search, and a debugging assistant, letting you instantly search for and insert code snippets from ChatGPT, Stack Overflow, GitHub, and more via a browser extension. It provides real-time code completion and error explanations directly within VS Code and JetBrains. A free plan is available for individuals, with a Pro plan for teams priced at $3.99/user/mo.

code-codium.summary: Automatically generates test cases so your code stays bug-free
code-codium.description: An AI test automation tool from Qodo (formerly CodiumAI) that analyzes your code and automatically generates unit and integration tests, including edge cases. It supports a variety of languages such as Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, and Java, and works as a plugin for VS Code and JetBrains. A free plan is available for individuals, with team plans starting at $19/user/mo.

code-sourcery.summary: Cleans up messy Python code with automatic refactoring
code-sourcery.description: An AI refactoring tool specialized for Python code that automatically detects unnecessary duplication, overly complex logic, and non-Pythonic patterns, then suggests cleaner, more efficient rewrites. It also includes a CI integration that automatically posts refactoring suggestions as comments on GitHub PRs to support code review. A free plan is available for individuals, with team plans priced at $12/user/mo.

code-mintlify.summary: Just write your code — Mintlify builds the docs for you
code-mintlify.description: A developer documentation platform that integrates with your GitHub codebase and uses AI to automatically generate comments and documentation for functions, APIs, and classes. Built on MDX for full version control, it powers the official documentation sites of thousands of tech companies, including Stripe, Anthropic, and Resend. Plans start at $150/mo for the Starter tier.

code-adrenaline.summary: Skip Stack Overflow — this debugger fixes your code directly
code-adrenaline.description: An AI debugging assistant that analyzes an error message and code you paste in, then instantly proposes a fix. It goes beyond simple error correction to understand your code's overall logic and suggest broader improvements. Service availability has been inconsistent lately, and similar functionality is now built into tools like Cursor and Copilot Chat.

code-figstack.summary: Turns baffling code into plain-English explanations
code-figstack.description: A code-explanation tool that takes a confusing snippet and has AI break it down in clear natural language. Beyond explanations, it also converts code between languages, auto-generates documentation, and analyzes code complexity. Available as a VS Code extension, it's especially useful for coding beginners and developers trying to make sense of someone else's code.

code-kodezi.summary: A great auto-fix tool for students learning to code
code-kodezi.description: An AI coding assistant built for coding beginners and students, offering automatic bug fixes, code generation, optimization, and documentation all in one place. It runs as a VS Code plugin and supports major languages like Python, JavaScript, Java, and C++. The free plan covers the essentials well, making it a solid pick for anyone just starting to learn to code.

code-safurai.summary: A local-first coding assistant with no privacy worries
code-safurai.description: A privacy-first AI coding assistant that never sends your code to an external server, offering autocomplete, code explanations, bug fixes, and test generation. It supports plugins for VS Code, JetBrains, Sublime Text, and other major IDEs. A free plan is available, along with private enterprise deployment options.

code-askcodi.summary: A coding Q&A bot you can use right inside Notion or VS Code
code-askcodi.description: An AI assistant built specifically for coding questions, available as a VS Code plugin and a web app. It handles code generation, explanation, debugging, and unit test writing, and supports over 30 programming languages. Its Workspaces feature lets teams build a shared knowledge base. Offered with a free plan and a Pro plan at $9.99/mo.

code-whatthediff.summary: Summarizes your PRs so code review takes less time
code-whatthediff.description: A code review automation tool that has AI analyze your GitHub pull requests and automatically post a comment summarizing the changes, flagging potential bugs, calling out security vulnerabilities, and suggesting improvements. Reviewers can grasp the essence of a change without reading through the entire diff. Plans start at $19/mo for the Starter tier, with GitHub Actions integration included.

voice-elevenlabs.summary: The most natural-sounding voice cloning and generation around today
voice-elevenlabs.description: An industry-leading AI voice synthesis and cloning platform that can clone a person's voice from less than a minute of sample audio and generate natural speech in 32 languages. It supports emotion control, tone adjustment, and real-time voice conversion, and is widely used for producing audiobooks, podcasts, and YouTube narration. Plans range from a free tier (10,000 characters/mo) up to the Creator plan at $22/mo.
pros: Best-in-class natural-sounding voices
Clones a voice from under a minute of sample audio
Emotion control across 32 languages
cons: Free plan is quite limited at 10,000 characters/mo
Paid plans are pricier than comparable services

voice-murf.description: A professional narration platform offering over 120 AI voices in 20 languages, with fine-grained control over emphasis, pace, and pitch. It includes a video editor for laying voice directly over footage, plus team collaboration and custom brand voice creation. Plans scale from a free tier (10 minutes/mo) up to the Creator plan at $29/mo.
pros: 120 AI voices across 20 languages
Built-in video editing
Team collaboration and custom brand voices
cons: Free plan capped at just 10 minutes/mo
Less natural-sounding than ElevenLabs

voice-playht.summary: Expressive AI voices with real-time conversation support
voice-playht.description: A large-scale voice synthesis platform with over 800 AI voices spanning 130 languages and dialects, offering precise control over emotion, pace, and intonation. Its 'PlayDialog' technology enables real-time voice conversation, making it suitable for chatbots and customer service automation. Plans range from a free tier (12,500 characters/mo) up to the Creator plan at $31.20/mo, with API access available.
pros: Largest lineup around: 800+ voices, 130 languages
Real-time voice conversation via PlayDialog
API access makes integration easy
cons: Free plan limited to 12,500 characters/mo
The sheer number of options can overwhelm beginners

voice-openai.summary: That ChatGPT voice, now available via API
voice-openai.description: OpenAI's text-to-speech API gives developers direct access to the six voices heard in ChatGPT Voice — Alloy, Echo, Fable, Onyx, Nova, and Shimmer. Fast generation and natural intonation are its strengths, and it's frequently paired with Whisper STT to build voice conversation apps. Priced at roughly $0.015 per 1,000 characters (HD tier).

voice-speechify.summary: Built for dyslexia, it reads anything out loud
voice-speechify.description: A text-to-speech service that has AI naturally read aloud any text — web pages, PDFs, emails, Google Docs — originally created to improve accessibility for people with dyslexia. Playback speeds up to 4.5x let you consume content far faster than reading it yourself. Available on iOS, Android, and as a Chrome extension, with a Premium plan at $139/mo.

voice-lovo.summary: Emotionally expressive AI voiceovers for video creators
voice-lovo.description: A narration-focused platform offering over 500 AI voices in 100 languages, with fine controls for expressing emotions like joy, sadness, and anger. Its built-in 'Genny' video editor handles everything from script writing to narration to final video editing in one place. Plans start at $24/mo for the Starter tier.
pros: Huge selection: 500 voices, 100 languages
Fine-grained emotional expression control
Built-in video editing via Genny
cons: Starter plan at $24/mo runs on the pricier side
Some learning curve to the UI

voice-rvc.summary: Turn your voice into a famous singer or character (open source)
voice-rvc.description: Short for Retrieval-based Voice Conversion, this is an open-source AI voice conversion model that transforms your voice into someone else's using just a short sample. The community shares a wide range of voice models for celebrities, characters, and singers, making it popular for music covers and creative voice projects. It's free and open source, runnable on a local GPU or through web demos.

voice-overdub.summary: Trains on your voice so you can edit audio like fixing a typo
voice-overdub.description: A voice-cloning feature built into Descript: train it on about 10 minutes of your own voice, and afterward you can generate audio of you speaking just by typing text. It lets you swap out a misspoken line in a podcast or YouTube video by editing the transcript instead of re-recording. Available on Descript's Creator plan ($24/mo) and above.

voice-gcloud.summary: Google's engine — the most stable, with the widest language support
voice-gcloud.description: Google Cloud's TTS API supports over 50 languages and 220+ voices, delivering high-quality synthesized speech powered by WaveNet and Neural2. It offers enterprise-grade reliability with a 99.9% SLA and is used at massive scale in products like Google Maps, Google Assistant, and automated response systems. Free for up to 1 million characters/mo, then $0.000016 per character after that.

voice-typecast.summary: Korea's top service, with the most natural Korean pronunciation
voice-typecast.description: An AI voice platform from Korean startup Scatter Lab, widely regarded as delivering the most natural rendition of Korean intonation, pitch, and emotional nuance. It offers over 100 AI voice characters, each customizable for emotion and speaking style. Plans range from a free tier (2,000 characters/mo) up to the Pro plan at $19/mo.
pros: Best-in-class Korean pronunciation and intonation
Over 100 AI voice characters available
Free plan includes 2,000 characters/mo
cons: Global support is limited, given its Korea focus
Fewer language options than English-first competitors

voice-resemble.summary: A voice cloning tool with built-in deepfake detection
voice-resemble.description: An AI voice synthesis platform capable of high-quality voice cloning from just a 3-second sample, with support for real-time voice conversion and emotional expression control. Its proprietary 'Watermarking' technology embeds an inaudible watermark into generated audio to aid deepfake detection, reflecting a responsible-AI approach to voice synthesis. Offered as an enterprise API with custom pricing.

voice-wellsaid.summary: Enterprise-grade narration nearly indistinguishable from a human voice
voice-wellsaid.description: A high-quality AI narration platform built for corporate training, marketing, and e-learning content, producing voice so natural it's hard to tell apart from a human recording. It offers over 50 professional AI announcer voices along with custom pronunciation dictionaries and team collaboration features. Plans start at $49/mo for the Creator tier.

voice-replica.summary: AI voice acting built for game and film characters
voice-replica.description: An AI voice synthesis platform specialized in character dubbing for games, animation, and film, with fine-grained control over emotional intensity and performance style. It's a proven solution used in production by major studios like Epic Games, EA, and Warner Bros. for generating NPC dialogue. Plans start at $24/mo for the Starter tier, with SDKs for Unity and Unreal Engine integration.

voice-uberduck.summary: Fun voice generator that makes rapper voices spit bars
voice-uberduck.description: A creative platform combining AI voice synthesis with music generation, best known for producing covers and raps in the voice styles of famous rappers and artists. It offers a community library of over 5,000 shared voice models, widely used by music producers and creators for experimental content. Available with a free plan and a Pro plan at $9.99/mo.

voice-voicify.summary: A tool for making song covers in famous singers' voices
voice-voicify.description: A music-focused platform that uses AI voice conversion technology to create song covers in the voice style of any artist you choose. It offers a library of hundreds of AI artist models, and once you upload a song file, it generates a cover converted into the voice you want within minutes. Plans start at $9.99/mo with the Pro plan, mainly used by music fans for creative projects.

voice-kits.summary: A royalty-free AI voice library for musicians
voice-kits.description: An AI voice conversion platform for music creators that lets you legally produce and commercially use AI covers through royalty-free artist voice models. You can also upload your own voice to create a personal AI voice model and earn a share of revenue through its 'Artist Partner Program.' Available from a Free plan up to a Pro plan at $9.99/mo.

voice-altered.summary: A voice changer that turns your voice into a professional voice actor's
voice-altered.description: Real-time and offline AI voice conversion software that instantly transforms your microphone input into a range of professional voice-actor-quality character voices. It's used for podcasts, audiobooks, dubbing, and game voice recording, with natural-sounding tone conversion as its key strength. Plans start at $29/mo with the Starter plan, which also includes custom voice-training features.

voice-metavoice.summary: High-performance voice conversion that conveys emotion in real time
voice-metavoice.description: A startup offering a high-quality AI TTS model via API that naturally conveys emotion, giving it an edge in audio dramas, games, and conversational AI where emotional nuance matters. It can clone a voice from less than a second of audio sample, and open-source models are also publicly available. Delivered as an API and billed on a usage basis.

voice-voiceai.summary: Real-time voice modulation for Discord and games
