voice-voiceai.description: An AI voice changer app that modulates your voice in real time on Discord, Zoom, games, and more, letting you instantly apply thousands of voice effects — from robots and monsters to celebrity voices. It works with any app through a virtual microphone, requiring no separate audio interface, and is free to use on PC. Premium features require a $9.99/mo subscription.

voice-coqui.summary: The foundation of open-source voice synthesis: text-to-speech
voice-coqui.description: An open-source TTS framework originally started by Mozilla, providing the groundwork for researchers and developers to train and use custom voice synthesis models. While the company behind it has since shut down its service, the open-source codebase continues to be developed by the community and is widely cited as a standard benchmark in TTS research. Free to use on GitHub.

3d-meshy.summary: Enter text or an image and get a 3D model in a minute
3d-meshy.description: An AI platform that generates 3D models ready for immediate use in games and design within a minute, from a text description or an image. It supports export in various formats including OBJ, FBX, GLTF, and STL, and comes with automatic rigging so models are ready for animation. Available with a free plan (200 credits/mo) and a Pro plan starting at $20/mo.
pros: Fastest 3D model generation, under a minute
Supports multiple formats including OBJ, FBX, GLTF
Free plan includes 200 credits per month
cons: Limited mesh quality for complex characters
Extra credits required for high-resolution textures

3d-tripo.summary: A tool for pulling out 3D assets fast and lean
3d-tripo.description: An ultra-fast AI 3D generation platform that produces high-quality 3D models from images, text, or multi-view photos in under 8 seconds. It's used to create 3D assets for games, film, AR/VR, and e-commerce, and supports formats compatible with Blender, Maya, Unreal Engine, and more. Available with a free plan and a Basic plan starting at $7.99/mo.
pros: Ultra-fast 3D generation in under 8 seconds
Supports game-engine-compatible formats
Free plan available
cons: Limited at rendering complex organic shapes
A paid plan is needed for high quality

3d-csm.summary: Generates a 3D cube that rotates 360 degrees from just one photo
3d-csm.description: An AI 3D generation platform from Common Sense Machines (CSM) that automatically creates 3D models capable of full 360-degree rotation from a single image, multiple photos, or a text description. It's a solution that dramatically cuts the cost and time of producing 3D assets across fields like gaming, the metaverse, and e-commerce. Available via API and also as a web app.

3d-spline.summary: Design in 3D right in your browser and edit with AI
3d-spline.description: A 3D design and interaction tool that runs directly in the browser, letting you create interactive 3D content ready to embed in websites and apps through a Figma-like, intuitive interface. AI prompts let you instantly generate or modify 3D scenes, and it integrates easily with React, Webflow, Framer, and more. Available with a free plan and a Pro plan starting at $9/mo.
pros: Build 3D interactions right in the browser
Integrates with web frameworks like React and Webflow
Free plan plus an affordable Pro plan ($9)
cons: Not suited for complex, high-poly modeling
No offline support

3d-lumagenie.summary: 3D models whipped up with a quick Discord command
3d-lumagenie.description: Luma Labs' AI 3D model generation service lets you create 3D assets ready for games and scenes from text or images, using Discord bot commands or a web app. It uses NeRF-based 3D reconstruction technology, excelling at realistic texture rendering. Available with a free plan (30 credits/mo) and a Pro plan starting at $9.99/mo.
pros: Realistic texture rendering powered by NeRF
Easy access via a Discord bot
Free plan includes 30 credits per month
cons: Generation time is relatively slow
Limited accuracy on complex shapes

3d-rodin.summary: A high-quality model specialized in 3D figures and character generation
3d-rodin.description: An AI 3D character and figure generation model from Deemos that produces photorealistic, high-quality 3D human and creature models from text or images. With high mesh quality and outstanding texture detail, it's well-suited for game character and 3D-printed figure production. Available via API and web platform, with a free trial followed by a Pro plan starting at $30/mo.

3d-masterpiece.summary: Put on a VR headset and sculpt alongside AI
3d-masterpiece.description: An XR 3D creation platform that lets you sculpt 3D models in virtual space while wearing a VR headset like Meta Quest. It generates base shapes from AI text prompts, which you can then refine intuitively with VR controllers, making it popular among VR artists. Available starting with the Creator plan at $10/mo.

3d-leonardo.summary: Comes with a feature that wraps 2D art onto 3D textures
3d-leonardo.description: A 3D texturing feature from Leonardo.ai that automatically maps AI-generated 2D images onto 3D models to produce realistic textures. It can dramatically cut down texturing time for game and metaverse assets, and stands out for integrating image-generation AI directly into a 3D workflow. Included with a Leonardo.ai subscription (Free to $48/mo).

3d-shape.summary: An open-source 3D generation model built by OpenAI
3d-shape.description: An open-source 3D generation model released by OpenAI in 2023 that generates 3D meshes and point clouds directly from text or images. It's widely used for research and development, and is also referenced as foundational technology behind commercial AI 3D tools. Free to use on GitHub, though it requires a local GPU environment.
pros: Officially open-source and free from OpenAI
Usable for research, development, and custom fine-tuning
Converts both text and images into 3D
cons: Requires setting up a local GPU environment
Lower quality compared to commercial services

3d-blockade.description: A platform specialized in AI-generated 360-degree panoramic environment images (skyboxes) that creates ready-to-use backgrounds for games, VR, and the metaverse from just a text prompt, in a matter of seconds. It supports export in EXR and HDRI formats used by Unity, Unreal Engine, Blender, and more. Available with a free plan and a Pro plan starting at $7.99/mo.

3d-kaedim.summary: Upload a 2D image and it's instantly converted into a 3D model
3d-kaedim.description: A platform that automatically converts uploaded 2D images or concept art into optimized 3D meshes ready for immediate use in game engines. Its main customers are game studios, and it's used in pre-production pipelines to quickly turn an artist's concept into 3D. Offered via API and a web dashboard, operating on an enterprise pricing model.

3d-polycam.summary: Scan objects with your iPhone's LiDAR sensor to turn them into 3D
3d-polycam.description: An app that combines the LiDAR sensor on iPhone and iPad with AI to scan real objects or spaces and convert them into high-quality 3D models. It supports export in GLTF, OBJ, USDZ, and STL formats, making it widely used for 3D printing, AR, gaming, and architectural visualization. It also supports photogrammetry on Android, with a Pro plan starting at $9.99/mo.

3d-kiri.summary: A 3D scanning app that also works on Android
3d-kiri.description: A 3D scanning app built on photogrammetry technology that works on both iOS and Android. AI analyzes photos taken from multiple angles to automatically generate a precise 3D model, and it works even on regular smartphones without LiDAR. Available with a free plan and a Pro plan at $9.99/mo, supporting formats such as OBJ and GLTF.

3d-sloyd.summary: Specialized in generating 3D props (objects) for games
3d-sloyd.description: A 3D object generation tool optimized for game development that instantly creates low-poly or high-poly props with optimized polygon counts from text prompts. It supports formats ready to use directly in Unity and Unreal Engine, and specializes in producing in-game objects like buildings, furniture, weapons, and tools. Offers a free plan, with Pro plans starting at $20/mo.

3d-genie.summary: Luma Labs' tool for creating 3D models from text
3d-genie.description: An AI model developed by Luma Labs that converts text and images into 3D, generating high-quality 3D scenes based on NeRF and Gaussian Splatting from photos or text prompts. It's a core part of Luma Labs' product lineup alongside Dream Machine video generation, and is used to create 3D assets and background environments. Offers a free trial, with Pro plans starting at $9.99/mo.

3d-3dfy.summary: A solution for generating high-quality 3D models at scale
3d-3dfy.description: A large-scale 3D asset automation platform for e-commerce, gaming, and the metaverse, offering a pipeline that batch-converts hundreds or thousands of product images into 3D models. Its strengths are high mesh quality and PBR texture support, and its API-based architecture makes it easy to integrate into existing enterprise workflows. Operates on custom enterprise-only pricing.

3d-alpha3d.summary: Quickly creates 3D assets for augmented reality (AR)
3d-alpha3d.description: A platform that automatically generates 3D assets ready for AR, web, and games from 2D images, specializing in AR shopping (product previews) and interior visualization. It's an enterprise product adopted by major e-commerce companies like IKEA and Amazon as a product 3D visualization solution. Available via API and dashboard, with custom quote-based pricing.

3d-ponzu.summary: An AI tool for generating 3D textures (materials)
3d-ponzu.description: A tool that uses AI to automatically generate tileable PBR texture maps (Albedo, Normal, Roughness, AO) from text descriptions or reference images. It supports texture formats used in Blender, Unity, and Unreal Engine, dramatically cutting down texturing time when creating 3D models. Offers a free trial, with Pro plans starting at $19/mo.

3d-latent.summary: Another strong contender for generating 360-degree skybox images
3d-latent.description: A platform dedicated to AI-generated 360-degree panoramas and skybox images, quickly creating background scenes for games, VR, and metaverse environments from text prompts. It supports HDRI and EXR formats, so it can also be used as lighting environments in Blender and Unreal Engine. Offers a free plan, with Pro plans starting at $9/mo.

3d-csm-ai.summary: A platform that turns photos into 3D game assets
3d-csm-ai.description: Common Sense Machines' 3D asset generation platform automatically creates optimized 3D models, ready for use in games and the metaverse, from smartphone photos of real objects. Its strengths are 3D reconstruction accuracy and support for multiple formats, and it's mainly used by e-commerce, gaming, and AR/VR app developers. Available as an API and web app, with usage-based pricing.

auto-n8n.summary: An unlimited automation tool you can self-host for free
auto-n8n.description: An open-source workflow automation platform you can install and run on your own server for free. It integrates with 400+ apps and even supports nodes that execute JavaScript or Python code. Non-developers can build complex automations by connecting nodes on a visual canvas, and it's also used to build AI agent pipelines. Cloud plans start at €24/mo, while self-hosting is completely free.
pros: Fully free, open-source self-hosting
400+ integrations and AI agent pipeline support
Endlessly extensible with code-execution nodes
cons: Requires server management if self-hosted
Has a learning curve for beginners

auto-zapier.summary: The most famous and easiest automation tool, with the most app integrations
auto-zapier.description: The world's largest automation platform, supporting 7,000+ app integrations. It automatically connects data between apps using a no-code 'trigger → action' model. It supports AI-powered automatic Zap creation and multi-step automations, and is widely used from individuals to large enterprises. Starts with a free plan (100 tasks/mo), with the Professional plan at $69/mo.
pros: The most app integrations in the world, at 7,000+
The easiest no-code automation available
Offers AI-powered automatic Zap creation
cons: Paid plans are relatively expensive
Free plan is limited to 100 tasks/mo

auto-make.summary: Best for building complex visual workflows
auto-make.description: A visual automation platform supporting 1,600+ app integrations, letting you build complex logic like conditional branching, loops, and error handling in an intuitive flowchart format. It offers rich advanced data-transformation features like routing, aggregation, and filtering, making it well suited for users who need finer-grained automation than Zapier. Offers a Free plan (1,000 operations/mo), with Core plans starting at $9/mo.
pros: Visually build complex branching and looping logic
Rich advanced data-transformation features
Free plan includes 1,000 operations/mo
cons: Steeper learning curve than Zapier
Fewer app integrations than Zapier

auto-bardeen.summary: Unmatched for scraping web data with a single click
auto-bardeen.description: An AI automation tool in the form of a Chrome extension that automates repetitive browser tasks like web scraping, CRM data entry, email sending, and research, with no code required. GPT-powered AI understands your intent and generates automation recipes, and it integrates with major SaaS tools like HubSpot and Salesforce. Offers a free plan (14 credits/mo), with Pro plans starting at $10/mo.
pros: Strong browser automation via Chrome extension
AI automatically generates automation recipes
Integrates with major SaaS tools like HubSpot and Salesforce
cons: Limited to the Chrome browser only
Free plan is very limited, at just 14 credits/mo

auto-gumloop.summary: Specializes in building automation pipelines powered by AI (LLMs)
auto-gumloop.description: An automation builder specialized in integrating LLMs and AI models into workflows, letting you visually build AI agent pipelines by connecting various AI models like OpenAI, Claude, and Gemini as nodes. It excels at automations where AI handles web scraping, email processing, data classification, and content generation. Starter plans start at $97/mo.

auto-ifttt.summary: Ultra-simple, everyday 'if this, then that' automation
auto-ifttt.description: An everyday automation platform specialized in smart home, IoT, and mobile app automation, connecting 700+ smart devices and apps like Alexa, Google Home, and SmartThings. Its simple 'IF This Then That' recipe structure makes it easy for anyone to set up. Offers a free plan, with Pro plans starting at $2.99/mo.

auto-powerauto.summary: An essential tool for automating MS Office tasks like Excel and Outlook
auto-powerauto.description: An enterprise automation platform that integrates seamlessly with the Microsoft 365 ecosystem (Outlook, Excel, Teams, SharePoint, Dynamics 365). It offers both RPA capabilities for automating clicks and inputs in desktop apps, and cloud workflow features. Included with Microsoft 365 Business subscriptions, with standalone plans starting at $15/mo.
pros: Seamless integration with MS 365, Outlook, and Teams
RPA features for automating desktop apps
Strong on enterprise security and compliance
cons: Weaker integration outside the Microsoft ecosystem
Complex UI creates a barrier for beginners

auto-relay.summary: Great for automations that need human approval
auto-relay.description: An automation tool specialized in building semi-automated workflows where a person approves or reviews specific steps, rather than fully automating everything. Its strength lies in sending approval requests to the responsible person via Slack or email mid-workflow, then proceeding to the next step based on their response. Offers a Free plan, with Pro plans starting at $9/mo.

auto-pabbly.summary: A cost-effective automation tool with a one-time payment for lifetime use
auto-pabbly.description: An automation platform supporting 1,000+ app integrations that offers excellent value, since a one-time Lifetime Deal payment gives you unlimited use with no monthly fees. You can build various business automations — email marketing, payment processing, CRM, and more — all on one platform. Lifetime plans start at $249.

prod-gamma.summary: Magic that designs slides, documents, and websites in a minute
prod-gamma.description: A platform where you paste in text or a file and AI automatically applies design to generate a polished presentation, document, or webpage in under a minute. You can edit layout, colors, and content with natural language instructions alone, no mouse clicks needed, and collaborate through a single shareable link. Offers a free plan (400 AI credits/mo), with Plus plans starting at $10/mo.

auto-active.summary: A lightweight, open-source alternative to n8n
auto-active.description: An open-source workflow automation platform similar to n8n, letting you connect 200+ apps in a visual builder. Self-hosting is free, and it has built-in AI-powered automation step generation, making it easy for non-developers to use as well. Cloud plans start at $9/mo, and an active open-source community is one of its strengths.

auto-cheatlayer.summary: Uses GPT-4 to automate any website
auto-cheatlayer.description: A platform where GPT-4-powered AI converts natural-language commands into browser automation scripts, letting you automate any website without needing a separate API integration. AI directly handles data scraping, automatic form filling, and repetitive task processing, and even non-developers can build an automation just by describing the task in plain English. Starts at $199/mo.

auto-browse.summary: Detects website changes and extracts data
auto-browse.description: An AI web scraping automation tool that regularly extracts and monitors data from any website with no coding required. It automatically detects changes like price shifts, stock updates, or new job postings and sends alerts, then automatically sends the extracted data to Google Sheets, Zapier, and more. Offers a free plan (50 tasks/mo), with Starter plans starting at $19/mo.

auto-levity.summary: AI learns repetitive tasks like email sorting and handles them for you
auto-levity.description: A no-code AI automation platform that learns repetitive decision-making tasks—like image classification, text classification, and email routing—and handles them automatically. Train custom AI models on your own example data and connect them to existing automation tools like Zapier and Make. Starter plans start at $59/mo.

auto-axiom.summary: Records your browser actions and replays them as a macro
auto-axiom.description: A no-code browser automation tool built as a Chrome extension that records your browser actions—clicks, typing, scrolling—and replays them automatically. It makes it easy to automate data scraping, form filling, and repetitive tasks, and also supports advanced logic like conditional branching and loops. Available on a free plan, with Pro plans starting at $15/mo.

auto-hexomatic.summary: Web scraping and business automation in one place
auto-hexomatic.description: An all-in-one tool that handles web scraping and business automation on a single platform, letting you collect web data without coding and send it straight to Google Sheets, Zapier, Slack, and more. It offers over 100 automation recipes, including AI-powered data extraction, image analysis, and SEO monitoring. Starter plans start at $29/mo.

auto-robomotion.summary: Robotic process automation (RPA), made easy on the web
auto-robomotion.description: An RPA platform where bots handle repetitive tasks in cloud and desktop apps, with workflows built visually in a graphical editor. It supports automation across web, Windows, SAP, and other environments, with a low barrier to entry that makes it accessible even to non-developers. Available on a free plan (3 bots), with Pro plans starting at $35/mo.

auto-automa.summary: Open-source browser automation via a Chrome extension
auto-automa.description: An open-source browser automation tool available as a Chrome and Firefox extension, with workflows built in a block-based visual editor. It lets you automate tab management, data scraping, form filling, and scheduled runs without writing code, and every feature is free to use. Open-sourced and available on GitHub.

auto-uibot.summary: Screen-click automation that works on both Windows and Mac
auto-uibot.description: A Chinese-developed RPA platform and desktop-based automation tool that recognizes on-screen elements in Windows and macOS to automate repetitive tasks like clicking, typing, and copying. It uses OCR to read text and image recognition to locate UI elements, and is widely used by Chinese enterprises to automate SAP and ERP integrations. Available in a free version and paid enterprise plans.

auto-magical.summary: Complex data entry, automated like magic
auto-magical.description: A productivity tool delivered as a Chrome extension that handles repetitive data entry, text expansion, and autofill. Its core features let you fill out web forms—CRM records, support tickets, emails, and more—in one click, and expand short snippets into full sentences with text-expansion shortcuts. Available on a free plan, with Pro plans starting at $6.5/mo.

auto-harpa.summary: The ultimate Chrome extension for web page monitoring and automation
auto-harpa.description: A Chrome extension that brings AI and GPT capabilities into your everyday browsing, offering page summarization, price monitoring, automation, and search assistance. It's especially strong at web monitoring—AI analyzes and summarizes whatever page you have open, and sends alerts when specific conditions are met. Available on a free plan, with Pro plans starting at $15/mo.

music-suno.summary: Just add lyrics and out comes a radio-quality song (currently the #1 pick)
music-suno.description: An AI music platform that generates a complete song—vocals, instruments, and lyrics included—from a text prompt in seconds, widely regarded as the current industry leader in sound quality and naturalness. It supports a wide range of genres, from pop and hip-hop to jazz and classical, and generated tracks can be used commercially. Free plan (10 songs/mo), with Pro plans starting at $10/mo.
pros: Generates complete songs with vocals, lyrics, and instrumentation
Best-in-class sound quality and natural-sounding vocals
Free plan offers 10 songs/mo, with commercial use allowed
cons: Ongoing controversy over copyrighted training data
Lacks fine-grained music editing controls

music-udio.summary: A strong rival with polished musicality and powerful editing
music-udio.description: Considered one of the two leading platforms in AI music generation alongside Suno, its standout feature is 'Remix,' which stitches together 32-second clips or extends existing tracks. It offers fine-grained, music-theory-based control and high sound quality. Free plan (10 credits/mo), with Standard plans starting at $10/mo.
pros: Section stitching and Remix enable detailed editing
High sound quality with music-theory-based controls
Free plan includes 10 credits/mo
cons: Vocals sound slightly less natural than Suno's
Editing longer tracks is cumbersome due to the 32-second clip limit

music-stable.summary: Optimized for high-quality background music and sound effects
music-stable.description: A music platform built on Stability AI's audio generation model that creates high-quality background music up to 3 minutes long, as well as short sound effects, from text prompts. It stands out for its timestamp-based prompting, which lets you specify a different mood for each section of a track. Free plan (20 credits/mo), with Pro plans starting at $12/mo.

music-soundraw.summary: A custom BGM generator for YouTube, with zero copyright worries
music-soundraw.description: A royalty-free BGM generation platform for video creators—pick a genre, mood, tempo, and length, and AI instantly creates a custom soundtrack. Every track is cleared for use on YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, and more without copyright claims. Free plan (3 tracks/mo), with Creator plans starting at $19.99/mo.
pros: Copyright-claim-free BGM for YouTube and social media
Easy customization of genre, mood, and length
Free plan includes 3 tracks/mo
cons: Focused on instrumental BGM only—can't create full songs with vocals
Limited variety in generated music

music-riffusion.summary: Type a prompt, get a short musical riff
music-riffusion.description: A music-generation counterpart to image models like Stable Diffusion, using a novel approach that generates spectrograms—visual representations of sound—as images and converts them into music. It creates short melodies and riffs from text prompts, and first gained attention through a real-time music generation demo. Currently available as a free web app and an open-source model.

music-aiva.summary: Excels at emotive composition, from classical to film scores
music-aiva.description: The first AI composer officially registered with a European copyright society, this platform specializes in classical, film, and advertising music. Choose from over 200 styles to generate orchestral, piano, or chamber music ranging from one minute to several minutes long. Free plan (3 tracks/mo), with Standard plans starting at $9.99/mo.
pros: Officially registered AI composer with a European copyright society
200+ styles specialized in classical and film music
Free plan includes 3 tracks/mo
cons: Limited when it comes to pop and mainstream genres
Generation times are relatively slow

music-mubert.summary: Real-time generated background music for streaming and games
music-mubert.description: A real-time, adaptive AI music generation platform that instantly produces seamless, continuous background music tailored to the mood of streaming, gaming, podcasts, and more. It can generate royalty-free music of unlimited length, making it ideal for creators who need looping BGM. API integration is also available, with a free plan and Artist plans starting at $14/mo.
pros: Seamless real-time BGM for streaming and games
Generates royalty-free music of unlimited length
Supports API integration
cons: Can't generate songs with vocals or lyrics
Limited fine-grained control over musical style

music-boomy.summary: Even beginners can make a song in seconds and release it on Spotify
music-boomy.description: An all-in-one platform where AI automatically composes songs and lets you distribute them directly to major streaming services like Spotify, Apple Music, and Amazon Music. No musical knowledge required—just pick a genre and get a finished song in seconds. Free plan available, with Creator plans starting at $9.99/mo; Boomy takes a share of streaming revenue.

music-beatoven.summary: BGM generation with emotion tuned to your video's mood
music-beatoven.description: A platform developed by an Indian startup that generates royalty-free background music matched to your video's mood, emotion, and genre. You can fine-tune emotional parameters—like joy, sadness, or mystery—to customize the music's feel exactly how you want it. Free plan (5 tracks/mo), with Pro plans starting at $19/mo.

music-loudly.summary: A massive AI music library paired with a generation engine
music-loudly.description: A platform that combines a library of over 1,000 AI music tracks across genres and moods with a real-time music generation engine. Use sliders to adjust tempo, energy, and mood parameters, or edit existing tracks to customize them further. Free plan available, with Pro plans starting at $9/mo.

music-ecrett.summary: Intuitive BGM generation built for video creators
music-ecrett.description: A royalty-free AI music generation platform built for video creators—pick a scene, mood, and genre, and it instantly creates background music tailored to your footage. Tracks are cleared for use on YouTube, Vimeo, and social media without copyright claims, and the simple interface makes it easy to use even without any musical knowledge. Starter plans start at $17.99/mo.

music-cassette.summary: Your own beats, made with machine learning
music-cassette.description: A machine-learning-powered music production platform for creating custom beats and instrumentals—choose genre, instrument, and mood parameters to quickly produce your own beat. Generated tracks come with a commercially usable license. Free trial available, with Pro plans starting at $7.99/mo.

music-splash.summary: Generative AI from the company behind the famous Roblox music game
music-splash.description: An AI music generation platform from Splash, the company that rose to fame with its in-game music experience on Roblox. It quickly produces finished pop, hip-hop, and dance tracks from just a text description. It excels at creating short clips for games and short-form video, and generated music can be used commercially. Offered with a free plan and Pro plans starting at $7.99/mo.

music-musiclm.summary: A high-performance music AI Google released for research purposes
music-musiclm.description: An AI music generation model developed by Google Research that produces music across a wide range of genres and instrumentations from text prompts, released for academic and research purposes. It was offered experimentally through the 'AI Test Kitchen' platform and access is now limited, but it went on to lay the groundwork for Google's later commercial music AI technology.

music-songr.summary: A free site that turns your lyrics into a sung song
music-songr.description: A free AI songwriting platform that takes your lyrics as text input and has AI compose a melody and sing it. You can customize the style by choosing a genre and mood, letting anyone create their own song without any musical knowledge. Core features are free, with paid plans available for high-quality downloads.

music-voicemod.summary: Built-in AI Singer feature that turns text into song
music-voicemod.description: An AI Singer feature added to Voicemod, the platform known for real-time voice changing, that lets you enter text and generate content sung in a virtual character's voice. It's a unique platform combining real-time voice effects — used in gaming, streaming, and music production — with voice synthesis technology. Offered with a free plan and Pro plans starting at $7.99/mo.

music-koe.summary: Transforms your voice into a different vocal style
music-koe.description: An AI voice conversion tool that lets you transform your voice into a different gender, age, or character style, or restyle your singing voice entirely. It supports both real-time conversion and offline audio file processing, and is used for music creation and voice content production. Offered with a free plan and Pro plans starting at $4.99/mo.

music-voiceswap.summary: Turn your demo track into an artist's voice (legally)
music-voiceswap.description: A legal AI voice conversion service offered with artists' consent, letting you convert your demo track or music into the voice of a registered artist. It's built around a fair revenue-sharing model that benefits both artists and users. Pro plans start at $9.99/mo.

music-moises.summary: Separates vocals, drums, and other instruments from any track (the best in class)
music-moises.description: An AI-powered stem separation platform that isolates and extracts individual tracks — vocals, drums, bass, piano, guitar, and more — from a finished song. It also offers a smart metronome, key transposition, and pitch adjustment, making it hugely popular as a music practice app too. Offered with a free plan (5 songs/mo) and Pro plans starting at $3.99/mo.

music-lalal.summary: High-quality stem separation and vocal removal
music-lalal.description: A platform specializing in AI stem separation, capable of splitting a track into up to 10 high-quality stems, including vocals, drums, bass, and piano. It's known for industry-leading separation quality and is widely used by music producers, DJs, and cover artists. Offered with a free plan (10-minute preview) and pay-as-you-go pricing ($15 in credits).

music-splitter.summary: Fine-grained instrument separation from 2 to 5 stems
music-splitter.description: An AI stem separation tool offering granular splitting options, from a simple 2-stem vocal/instrumental split up to a full 5-stem breakdown of vocals, drums, bass, piano, and guitar. It supports both a web app and an API, making it suitable for individual users and businesses alike. Offered with a free plan (3 songs/mo) and Premium plans starting at $7.99/mo.

music-audioshake.description: An enterprise-grade AI stem separation platform built for the broadcast, film, and music industries, capable of handling studio-quality vocal and instrument separation and remix preparation at scale. It's a B2B solution actually used by major media companies like YouTube, Spotify, and Warner Music. It supports API access and large-scale batch processing, and runs on custom enterprise pricing.

ppt-gamma.summary: Just give it a topic and get a fully designed deck (highly recommended)
ppt-gamma.description: A tool where you simply enter a topic and AI automatically builds a complete presentation — content, design, and images included. Beyond slides, it can also convert output into document or webpage formats, making it useful for a wide range of purposes. Offered with a free plan (400 AI credits/mo) and Plus plans starting at $10/mo.
pros: Generates a complete deck automatically from just a topic
Can also convert output into document or webpage formats
Free plan includes 400 AI credits per month
cons: Limited fine-grained layout customization
Limited PowerPoint compatibility

ppt-tome.description: A storytelling-focused presentation tool: give it a topic and AI automatically generates a slide deck built around a narrative structure. It offers a more visual and interactive format than traditional PPT, seamlessly integrating embedded video and data visualizations. Offered with a Free plan and Pro plans starting at $16/mo.
pros: Excellent automatic narrative structuring
Seamlessly integrates video and data visualizations
Free plan available
cons: Weak PPT/Keynote export functionality
Somewhat limited editing flexibility

ppt-beautiful.summary: Lays out slides like a pro, even with zero design sense
ppt-beautiful.description: The 'DesignAI' engine automatically optimizes layout, fonts, and color every time you add content to a slide, producing professional-looking results even if you have no design experience. It supports template-based creation and team brand kit management. Offered with a free plan and Pro plans starting at $12/mo.
pros: AI automatically optimizes layout, fonts, and color
Supports team brand kit management
Free plan available
cons: $12/mo Pro plan is a barrier to entry
Weaker AI content generation than Gamma

ppt-slidesai.summary: Turns text into slides right inside Google Slides
ppt-slidesai.description: An AI presentation plugin that integrates directly with Google Slides — enter text or a topic and AI automatically generates the slide content and design. Existing Google Slides users can start using it right after installing, with no need to switch tools. Offered with a free plan (3 presentations/mo) and Basic plans starting at $10/mo.
pros: Integrates directly as a Google Slides plugin
Works within your existing setup, no tool switching needed
Free plan includes 3 presentations per month
cons: Google Slides only — no support for other platforms
AI design quality lags behind Gamma

ppt-pitch.summary: A modern presentation tool combining collaboration and AI generation
ppt-pitch.description: A modern slide tool combining team collaboration with AI presentation generation, letting multiple people edit simultaneously just like Google Docs. It offers over 60 design themes plus AI content generation, and is widely used by startup and consulting teams. Offered with a Free plan and Pro plans starting at $8/mo.
pros: Strong real-time team collaboration and editing
Optimized for startup and consulting teams
Free plan available
cons: AI auto-generation features are fairly basic
Less well-known than Gamma or Tome

ppt-napkin.summary: Turns text into cute, hand-drawn-style diagrams
ppt-napkin.description: A visual thinking tool where you enter a text description and AI automatically generates a flowchart, diagram, or mind map. It supports both a hand-drawn style and a clean digital style, and specializes in quickly visualizing complex concepts. Offered with a Free plan and Pro plans starting at $8/mo.

ppt-decktopus.summary: AI that even drafts your speaker script and anticipated Q&A
ppt-decktopus.description: An all-in-one presentation prep tool: AI automatically generates your deck and also prepares speaker notes and anticipated Q&A questions. It offers over 500 templates and AI image generation, plus link sharing and embedding. Starter plans start at $9.99/mo.

ppt-msdesigner.summary: Instantly generate PPT covers and images with AI
ppt-msdesigner.description: An AI design tool from Microsoft that instantly generates PowerPoint covers, social media images, ad banners, and more from just a text description. It's built on DALL-E 3 image generation and integrates closely with the Microsoft 365 ecosystem (PowerPoint, Teams, Bing). Included by default for Microsoft 365 subscribers, and also free to use as a standalone app.

ppt-prezi.summary: AI text generation added to Prezi's signature zoom effects
ppt-prezi.description: Prezi, known for its distinctive zoom-based spatial navigation, now has AI features that support text generation, image recommendations, and design automation. Its striking presentation style makes it popular for education and conference talks, and AI helps visualize complex ideas as hierarchical structures. Offered with a free Basic plan and Standard plans starting at $7/mo.

ppt-popai.summary: Turns a paper or PDF into a summarized presentation
ppt-popai.description: A tool that lets you upload a paper, PDF, or Word file and have AI extract the key content and automatically convert it into presentation slides. It also includes an AI chat feature for querying document contents in plain text, making it useful for reviewing papers and summarizing reports too. Offered with a free plan (3 uses/mo) and Pro plans starting at $9.99/mo.

ppt-sendsteps.summary: Create interactive presentations that engage your audience in real time
ppt-sendsteps.description: An interactive presentation platform that lets you engage your audience in real time with live polls, Q&A, quizzes, and word clouds. AI automatically generates quiz questions and poll items tailored to your topic, cutting down prep time. Available on a free Basic plan, with Pro plans starting at $14/mo.

ppt-slido.summary: The go-to tool for running live polls and Q&A during presentations
ppt-slido.description: An audience engagement platform acquired by Cisco that lets you run live polls, Q&A, quizzes, and word clouds during presentations. It integrates with one click into major presentation tools like Google Slides, PowerPoint, Prezi, and Zoom, making it the most widely used audience-engagement tool for corporate events, training, and webinars. Available on a free plan (up to 100 participants), with Pro plans starting at $15/mo.

ppt-deckrobot.summary: Automatically aligns your slides with your company's brand guidelines
ppt-deckrobot.description: An enterprise PowerPoint add-in that learns your company's brand guidelines — colors, fonts, logo placement — and automatically reformats any deck to match. It lets every team member produce on-brand presentations without needing a designer, making it popular with large enterprises. Available through custom enterprise pricing only.

ppt-slidebean.summary: Purpose-built for creating startup pitch decks to raise funding
ppt-slidebean.description: A platform specialized in building startup pitch decks for fundraising, where AI automatically structures your company information into an investor-friendly narrative. It offers over 500 pitch deck templates and content suggestions informed by VC feedback. Available on a free plan, with paid plans starting at $29/mo.

ppt-powerpresenter.summary: Get real-time AI coaching while you present to sharpen your skills
ppt-powerpresenter.description: A presentation coaching tool that analyzes your pace, clarity, confidence, and tone in real time while you rehearse, then delivers feedback for improvement. Repeated practice sessions help you systematically build stronger presentation skills, and it's also used to help overcome presentation anxiety. Available on a free plan, with Pro plans starting at $12/mo.

ppt-prezent.summary: An intelligent presentation platform built for the enterprise
ppt-prezent.description: An enterprise presentation platform used by Fortune 500 companies, featuring audience analysis, preference detection, and personalized content recommendations. AI-driven storyline suggestions and automated brand compliance help large teams maintain consistent presentations at scale. Available through custom enterprise pricing.

ppt-visme.summary: A powerhouse for infographics and data visualization
ppt-visme.description: A multi-format design tool that lets you create infographics, data visualizations, presentations, reports, and social graphics all in one platform. It comes loaded with thousands of templates plus AI text and image generation, along with an extensive library of charts, maps, and icons. Available on a free plan, with Standard plans starting at $12.25/mo.

ppt-zoho.summary: A free presentation tool built for real-time team collaboration
ppt-zoho.description: A free online presentation tool included in the Zoho productivity suite, compatible with PowerPoint and Keynote files and supporting real-time simultaneous editing by multiple users. It offers AI-generated speaker notes and design suggestions, and integrates tightly with the Zoho ecosystem, including Zoho CRM, Mail, and Writer. Available on the free Zoho plan.

ppt-haikudeck.summary: Quickly craft simple, emotionally resonant slides
ppt-haikudeck.description: A tool specialized in creating visually striking slides built around beautiful background images and minimal text, with AI automatically recommending on-topic images from a library of over 30,000 free photos. It's popular among educators and speakers who want to quickly put together minimal, visually striking presentations. Available on a free plan, with Pro plans starting at $9.99/mo.

ppt-sway.description: A web-based storytelling tool from Microsoft that weaves text, images, videos, tweets, and other content into a scrollable, interactive page — perfect for building presentations, newsletters, and reports. It's free to use with a Microsoft 365 account and integrates with SharePoint for publishing intranet content.

meet-otter.summary: The pioneer in English meeting recording and real-time transcription
meet-otter.description: The original AI meeting notes platform, transcribing and summarizing Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams calls in real time. It offers speaker identification, keyword highlighting, live captions, and team sharing, with exceptionally high accuracy for English speech recognition. Available on a free plan (600 minutes/mo), with Pro plans starting at $16.99/mo.
pros: The trusted original in AI meeting notes
Speaker identification and live captions
Free plan includes 600 minutes/mo
cons: English-focused, with lower accuracy for Korean
Free plan capped at 600 minutes/mo

meet-lilys.summary: Summarizes long YouTube videos or recordings into blog-style posts (Korean-made)
meet-lilys.description: An AI content summarization tool built by a Korean startup that analyzes YouTube URLs, audio files, and videos to generate structured, blog-post-style summaries. It excels at Korean speech recognition and summary quality, and is optimized for quickly grasping the key points of long lectures, meetings, and seminars. Available on a free plan, with Pro plans starting at $9.9/mo.

meet-fireflies.summary: Automatically joins Zoom, Google Meet, and other video calls to take notes
meet-fireflies.description: A meeting automation platform where an AI bot automatically joins your Zoom, Google Meet, Teams, or Webex calls to handle recording, transcription, meeting summaries, and action item extraction. It integrates with 30+ apps, including Slack, Notion, HubSpot, and Salesforce, to automatically share meeting content. Available on a free plan (800 minutes/mo), with Pro plans starting at $10/mo.
pros: AI bot automatically joins, records, and summarizes meetings
Auto-integrates and shares with 30+ apps
Free plan includes 800 minutes/mo
cons: The bot joining the call can feel intrusive
Korean recognition accuracy lags behind English

meet-tldv.summary: Records meetings and automatically tags key moments
meet-tldv.description: A meeting recording tool that captures video calls and timestamps key moments so you can quickly search and share them later. AI automatically extracts meeting summaries and next-step action items, and lets you clip specific moments into video snippets to share with your team. Available on a free plan (unlimited recording), with Pro plans starting at $18/mo.
pros: Free plan offers unlimited recording
Timestamping and video clip sharing
Automatic AI summaries and action item extraction
cons: Advanced features are locked behind the $18/mo Pro plan
Fewer app integrations than Fireflies

meet-fathom.summary: A free recording and summary assistant for Zoom and Teams meetings
meet-fathom.description: A free, unlimited-use AI meeting notes tool that automatically records Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams calls and generates summaries, transcripts, and action items. It can auto-sync meeting content to HubSpot, Salesforce, and Slack afterward, and even drafts follow-up emails with a single click. Free for individuals, with team plans at $19/user/mo.
pros: Unbeatable value — free and unlimited for individuals
One-click follow-up email drafts
Automatic syncing with HubSpot and Slack
cons: Team plan costs $19/user/mo
English-focused, with weaker Korean support

meet-krisp.summary: Eliminates background noise, like barking dogs, during calls
meet-krisp.description: An AI-powered real-time noise cancellation app that instantly removes background noise — keyboard clatter, AC hum, barking dogs, and more — from any calling app, including Zoom, Teams, Google Meet, and Discord. It delivers studio-quality audio purely through software, with no microphone upgrade required. Available on a free plan (60 minutes/week), with Pro plans starting at $8/mo.
pros: Eliminates noise perfectly in any calling app
Studio-quality audio through software alone
Free plan includes 60 minutes/week
cons: Focused solely on noise removal, with no real meeting-notes features
Free plan is very limited at just 60 minutes/week

meet-supernormal.summary: Sends out a clean, formatted meeting recap the moment your call ends
meet-supernormal.description: A tool that has AI automatically record your Google Meet, Zoom, and Teams calls, then emails participants a structured recap — summary, action items, and decisions — right after the meeting ends. It integrates with Notion, Slack, HubSpot, Jira, and more to automatically feed meeting content into your workflow tools. Available on a free plan (20 meetings/mo), with Pro plans starting at $18/mo.

meet-daglo.summary: A transcription tool with outstanding Korean speech recognition
meet-daglo.description: An AI speech recognition platform specialized for Korean, transcribing Korean meetings, lectures, and interviews with high accuracy. It offers speaker identification, paragraph segmentation, and automatic summarization, and is widely used by Korean startups and businesses for meeting transcription. Available on a free plan, with paid plans starting at ₩9,900/mo.

meet-meetgeek.summary: Analyzes meetings to surface insights on your team's strengths and weaknesses
meet-meetgeek.description: A meeting analytics platform that goes beyond simple note-taking to analyze your team's meeting patterns — who talks the most, how efficient meetings are, key keyword trends — and surface actionable insights. It automatically joins Zoom, Google Meet, and Teams calls, and integrates with HubSpot, Slack, and Notion. Available on a free plan (5 meetings/mo), with Pro plans starting at $19/mo.

meet-speakapp.summary: iPhone call recording and AI transcription in one app (Korean-made)
meet-speakapp.description: An app developed in Korea that works around iPhone's call-recording restrictions to record your calls, with AI transcribing and summarizing them. It automatically logs business calls, customer consultations, and other important conversations, storing them in a searchable format. Available via subscription on the Korean App Store.

meet-jamie.summary: Lives in your Mac menu bar, summarizing every meeting
meet-jamie.description: A tool that sits in the macOS menu bar and automatically detects any video call or voice call to generate AI meeting notes. It isn't tied to any single app — it automatically processes audio from any source and delivers a structured summary, decisions, and action items as soon as the meeting ends. Free plan (10 meetings/month) and Pro plan starting at €24/month.

meet-beulr.summary: Sends a bot to your Zoom meeting so you can skip it
meet-beulr.description: A tool that sends an AI bot into your Zoom meetings on your behalf and hands you a recording and summary afterward, so you can catch up without ever attending. It also offers automatic attendance, recording, and transcript generation for recurring meetings. Freemium, with core features free to use.

meet-avoma.summary: Meeting notes built for sales calls and CRM
meet-avoma.description: A sales-focused AI meeting assistant that records sales calls, auto-updates your CRM, analyzes customer sentiment, and recommends next steps. It integrates tightly with HubSpot, Salesforce, Zoom, and Google Meet, and includes analytics for sales coaching and pipeline management. Starter plan from $19/user per month.

meet-grain.summary: Great for sharing and analyzing customer interview footage
meet-grain.description: A tool built for recording video meetings and generating transcripts and highlight clips, making it easy to share and analyze UX research, customer interviews, and sales calls with your team. Its strength lies in clipping key moments to share and surfacing common insights across multiple interviews. Free plan (5 calls/month) and Starter plan from $15/month.

meet-assembly.summary: A powerful speech-recognition API for developers
meet-assembly.description: A speech AI API platform for developers, delivering a full suite of audio analysis capabilities via API — high-accuracy speech-to-text, speaker diarization, sentiment analysis, automatic chaptering, and PII redaction. Known for outperforming OpenAI Whisper on accuracy and offering more advanced features. Free plan (100 minutes/month) and usage-based pricing ($0.37/hour).

meet-sybill.summary: Reads the other side's expressions and gestures for emotion analysis
meet-sybill.description: A sales-focused AI tool that analyzes a customer's facial expressions, gestures, and speech patterns in real time during sales meetings to gauge emotion, engagement, and buying intent. After the call, it automatically generates deal summaries, customer sentiment reports, and sales tips, then syncs everything to your CRM. Enterprise plan with custom pricing.

meet-nyota.summary: A meeting notes assistant that syncs seamlessly with Notion
meet-nyota.description: An AI meeting notes tool whose strength is auto-generating recordings, summaries, and action items, then syncing them directly to leading work tools like Notion, Jira, Linear, HubSpot, and Slack. It integrates with Google Calendar to automatically detect and prep for upcoming meetings. Free plan and Pro plan from $14/month.

meet-tactiq.summary: Captures Google Meet captions live and saves them
meet-tactiq.description: A Chrome extension that automatically captures and saves live captions from Google Meet, Zoom, Teams, and Webex, then generates an AI summary and action items. Saved transcripts can be exported instantly to Notion, Google Docs, or Slack, or queried directly in ChatGPT. Free plan (10 meetings/month) and Pro plan from $12/month.

meet-sembly.summary: Automatically organizes the action items decided in meetings
meet-sembly.description: A tool that uses AI to analyze Zoom, Google Meet, and Teams meetings, automatically sorting the output into minutes, summaries, decisions, action items, and risk flags. Its standout feature is action-item automation that clearly extracts who needs to do what, and by when. Free plan (15 meetings/month) and Teams plan from $20/month.

meet-airgram.summary: Handles meeting recording, transcripts, and sharing in one place
meet-airgram.description: A meeting management tool that combines video recording, automatic transcription, and AI summaries on a single platform, so you can share both the recording and the transcript with your team via one link. It integrates with Zoom, Google Meet, and Teams, and lets you search meeting content by timestamp. Free plan and Pro plan from $18/month.

etc-deepl.summary: The most natural AI translation, outclassing Google Translate
etc-deepl.description: An AI translation service whose neural machine translation delivers more natural results than Google Translate, with industry-leading quality especially between European languages. It supports translating entire document files — Word, PowerPoint, PDF — and offers a Chrome extension. Free plan (500,000 characters/month) and Pro plan from $8.74/month.
pros: More natural translation quality than Google Translate
Translates whole document files, not just text
Free plan includes 500,000 characters/month
cons: Asian-language translation quality trails its European languages
Korean output can fall short of Papago

etc-grammarly.summary: Polishes the grammar and tone of English emails and documents
etc-grammarly.description: An AI English writing assistant used by more than 30 million people, offering grammar correction, vocabulary upgrades, tone adjustment, and even plagiarism checks. It works in real time everywhere — Chrome, Word, Google Docs — and tailors its feedback to the type of document, whether it's an email, a report, or an academic paper. Free plan and Premium plan from $12/month.
pros: Real-time corrections everywhere — Chrome, Word, Google Docs
All-in-one grammar, vocabulary, tone, and plagiarism checking
A writing tool trusted by 30 million users
cons: English only — no Korean proofreading support
Premium features require a subscription of $12/month or more

etc-quillbot.summary: A paraphrasing tool that turns clunky sentences fluent
etc-quillbot.description: An AI paraphrasing, summarizing, and grammar-correction tool that turns awkward English sentences into smoother, more natural prose. It offers seven rewriting modes — Standard, Fluency, Formal, Creative, Shorten, Expand, and Academic — so you can reshape text to fit your purpose. Popular with students, writers, and translators; free plan and Premium plan from $9.95/month.

etc-chatpdf.summary: Upload a PDF and chat with it like a bot to get the gist
etc-chatpdf.description: A service that lets you upload a PDF and have an AI analyze it, then chat with it like a chatbot to get answers to your questions. Whatever the document — papers, contracts, reports, textbooks — you can quickly find and summarize the parts you actually need. Free plan (2 documents/month, 10MB limit) and Plus plan from $5/month.
pros: Free to start instantly, no installation required
Fast Q&A on papers, contracts, and reports
Affordable Plus plan at just $5/month
cons: Free plan capped at 2 documents/month and 10MB
Falls behind PDF.ai on complex, multi-document analysis

etc-excelbot.summary: An assistant that writes gnarly Excel formulas from a plain-English request
etc-excelbot.description: A tool that instantly generates Excel and Google Sheets formulas from a plain-language description of what you need. Get tricky formulas — VLOOKUP, INDEX-MATCH, deeply nested IFs — without knowing the syntax, or have it explain what an existing formula does. Free plan (5 uses/month) and Basic plan from $1.99/month.

etc-goblin.summary: Type "make dinner" and it breaks it down from grocery run to cooking
etc-goblin.description: A productivity tool that uses AI to automatically break overwhelming big tasks into small, doable steps, built for people with ADHD or executive-function challenges. Type in "make dinner" and it produces a concrete to-do list covering everything from grocery shopping to prepping ingredients to the cooking steps. Free to use.

etc-pdfai.summary: Analyzes documents like papers and contracts and finds the citations for you
etc-pdfai.description: A service for analyzing PDFs through conversation with an AI — ask a question to find what you need in a paper, legal contract, or financial report, and get an answer along with a precise citation pointing to its exact location in the source. It also supports uploading multiple documents at once for side-by-side analysis. Free plan (1 document/day) and Pro plan from $14/month.
pros: Delivers precise citations with exact source locations
Supports comparing multiple documents at once
More sophisticated document analysis than ChatPDF
cons: Free plan limited to 1 document per day
Pro plan at $14/month costs more than ChatPDF

etc-monica.summary: A sidebar AI assistant you can use anywhere in your browser
etc-monica.description: An all-in-one AI assistant living in a Chrome sidebar that lets you tap GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, and other models at once for web search, translation, summarizing, and writing. You can ask the AI directly about the page you're viewing, and it also handles image generation and PDF analysis. Free plan and Pro plan from $9/month.

etc-arc.summary: Search once and AI reads multiple sites to summarize the results
etc-arc.description: An AI search feature built into The Browser Company's Arc browser — enter a query and the AI analyzes content across multiple websites to instantly deliver one unified answer. It reinvents search by handing you a direct answer instead of a list of links. Free as an iOS app.

etc-copyai.summary: Automates commercial writing like marketing copy and blog posts
etc-copyai.description: An AI content marketing platform for marketing teams that automatically generates a wide range of content — blog posts, emails, social media copy, ad copy, and more. It features brand-voice learning, SEO optimization, and CRM integration, along with support for team collaboration and content workflow management. Starter plan from $49/month.

etc-notion.summary: Writing, summarizing, and translating, all inside Notion
etc-notion.description: Built directly into your Notion workspace, this AI handles writing, summarizing, translating, autocomplete, and Q&A analysis based on your existing notes, docs, and databases. You can use AI right inside the document you're working on without ever switching apps, and it can answer questions by referencing everything already stored in your team's knowledge base. Available for $8/user on top of your existing Notion subscription.
pros: AI Q&A grounded in your existing Notion docs
All-in-one writing, summarizing, and translation
Use AI right inside the doc, no app-switching needed
cons: Requires a Notion subscription, can't be used standalone
Adds $8 per user on top of your existing Notion plan

etc-mem.summary: An AI note app that organizes your thoughts for you
etc-mem.description: A note-taking app where AI automatically organizes, categorizes, and connects your notes, linking related notes together and letting you instantly find what you need later with natural-language search. It also offers 'Mem AI Chat,' a feature that lets you talk through and develop ideas based on your notes. Pro plans start at $14.99/month.

etc-rewind.summary: A tool that remembers and searches everything on your screen
etc-rewind.description: A 'second brain' tool for Mac that automatically records everything that appears on your screen — web pages, apps, meetings, documents — and lets you instantly find it again with AI-powered search. Search in natural language for things like 'that chart I saw last Tuesday' or 'that link from last week's meeting.' All data is stored locally, and Pro plans start at $19/month.

etc-taskade.summary: From project management to mind maps, all handled by AI
etc-taskade.description: An all-in-one productivity platform combining AI-powered project management, to-do lists, mind maps, document writing, and team chat. Describe a project in natural language and AI automatically breaks it down into tasks and schedules them. Free plan available, with Pro plans starting at $8/user/month.

etc-todoist.summary: AI analyzes your to-do list and recommends priorities
etc-todoist.description: A popular to-do app used by 30 million people, where AI analyzes deadlines, importance, and workload to automatically recommend what you should focus on today. Add tasks quickly with natural-language input, and connect with major apps like Google Calendar, Gmail, and Slack. Free plan available, with Pro plans starting at $4/month.

etc-morgen.summary: Unify all your calendars and manage your schedule with AI
etc-morgen.description: An AI calendar app that unifies Google Calendar, Outlook, Apple Calendar, and more into a single view. An AI scheduling assistant automatically analyzes everyone's availability to suggest the best meeting times, and it also supports focus-time blocking to keep work and personal schedules separate. Free plan available, with Pro plans starting at $9/month.

etc-reclaim.summary: Finds open time slots and auto-schedules work and rest
etc-reclaim.description: A smart scheduler that syncs with Google Calendar, analyzes your tasks, habits, and meetings, and lets AI automatically place them at the optimal times. Enter things like work deadlines, workouts, or lunch breaks, and AI will slot them into open spaces on your calendar and resolve conflicts automatically. Free plan (4 calendars) available, with Starter plans starting at $10/month.

etc-clockwise.summary: Coordinates your team's schedules to protect focus time
etc-clockwise.description: A team calendar optimization tool where AI analyzes your whole team's Google Calendars to cut unnecessary meetings and automatically carve out deep-work time. It auto-resolves meeting conflicts and analyzes team availability to suggest the best possible meeting times. Free plan (for individuals) available, with Teams plans starting at $6.75/user/month.

etc-motion.summary: An intelligent calendar that plans your day automatically
etc-motion.description: An intelligent calendar app where you enter your tasks, meetings, and project deadlines, and AI reorganizes your calendar in real time to auto-arrange your day for optimal use of time. If a sudden meeting gets added or a task runs long, it automatically reshuffles the rest of your schedule. Individual plans start at $19/month.

etc-trevor.summary: An easy drag-and-drop tool for planning your day
etc-trevor.description: An intuitive scheduling tool that pulls in tasks from Todoist, Google Calendar, ClickUp, Trello, and more, then lets you drag and drop them into a time-blocked daily plan. AI analyzes task importance and estimated duration to suggest how your day should be laid out. Free plan available, with Pro plans starting at $3.99/month.

mkt-jasper.summary: The pioneer of marketing copywriting, learns your brand voice
mkt-jasper.description: The original AI marketing copywriting platform, it learns a company's brand guidelines, voice, and style to generate blog posts, ads, and email copy in a consistent tone. It offers over 50 content templates spanning SEO content, social posts, product descriptions, and email campaigns. Creator plans start at $49/month.
pros: Learns your brand voice for consistent tone
50+ content templates included
The industry-standard AI marketing copywriter
cons: Starting at $49/month, pricey for individual users
Korean output quality lags behind English

mkt-surfer.summary: Real-time writing guidance to help you rank on Google
mkt-surfer.description: An SEO-focused platform for writing content that ranks on Google, analyzing top-ranking competitor pages to give you real-time guidance on which keywords to use and how often. Its AI Writer auto-generates SEO-optimized article drafts, and it also includes keyword research and site audit features. Essential plans start at $89/month.
pros: Real-time SEO guidance based on competitor analysis
Automatic keyword density and topic recommendations
All-in-one platform for SEO blog writing
cons: At $89/month, steep for solo bloggers
Korean SEO data is thinner than English data

mkt-adcreative.summary: Auto-generates hundreds of high-CTR ad banners and copy
mkt-adcreative.description: A performance-ad platform where AI automatically mass-generates high-click-through, high-conversion ad banners and copy. Trained on millions of ad data points, it provides predictive performance scores for which visual and copy combinations are likely to perform best. Starter plans start at $21/month, and it supports Facebook, Google, and LinkedIn ad formats.
pros: Predictive performance scores help pick the best banner
Supports Facebook, Google, and LinkedIn formats
Conversion optimization trained on millions of ads
cons: Built specifically for ad banners, not general content
Starts at $21/month but credits run out fast

mkt-semrush.summary: From competitor analysis to keyword recommendations, all SEO covered
mkt-semrush.description: The world's largest digital marketing analytics platform, covering SEO, SEM, social analytics, and competitive intelligence, with a database of over 10 billion keywords plus competitor ad and backlink analysis. An all-in-one marketing tool that integrates AI-powered content optimization, social media scheduling, and ad performance tracking. Pro plans start at $129.95/month.
pros: 10-billion-keyword database with competitor ad analysis
All-in-one platform covering SEO, SEM, and social
The largest digital marketing dataset in the industry
cons: At $129.95/month, expensive for startups
So feature-dense that beginners face a steep learning curve

mkt-predis.summary: Generates Instagram/TikTok videos and posts all at once
mkt-predis.description: A social media content automation platform where AI analyzes your product or keywords to automatically generate images, videos, and captions optimized for Instagram, TikTok, Twitter, and LinkedIn. It analyzes competitor content to suggest winning strategies, and also includes post scheduling. Free plan (15 credits/month) available, with Starter plans starting at $27/month.

mkt-anyword.summary: Predicts which copy will drive clicks before you publish
mkt-anyword.description: A data-driven copywriting platform that gives you a predicted performance score before AI even generates your marketing copy. Trained on millions of ad data points, its model predicts which copy will drive clicks and conversions, and it continuously improves based on A/B test results. Starter plans start at $49/month.
pros: Predicted performance scores before you write the copy
Continuously improves using A/B test results
Covers ad, email, and social copy across every channel
cons: Starting at $49/month, no price advantage over Jasper
Korean performance prediction accuracy is limited

mkt-scalenut.summary: Handles long-form blog writing and SEO optimization together
mkt-scalenut.description: An AI-powered SEO content platform that covers the entire content workflow — keyword research, content ideation, long-form blog writing, optimization, and publishing. It analyzes top-ranking competitor pages to automatically recommend the keywords and topics you need to rank. Essential plans start at $39/month.

mkt-marketmuse.summary: Analyzes your content's authority and competitiveness to build strategy
mkt-marketmuse.description: An AI platform for content strategy that analyzes your site's content gaps, competitiveness, and topic authority to recommend which topics to tackle first for maximum SEO impact. It also includes AI-generated content briefs and AI writing tools. Standard plans start at $149/month.

mkt-writer.summary: Enterprise marketing writing with security and compliance built in
mkt-writer.description: An enterprise AI writing platform adopted by Fortune 500 companies, which learns a company's brand guidelines, banned terms, and legal requirements so every employee can produce content in a consistent tone. It runs its own LLM (Palmyra), meaning no data is sent to outside models, making it especially well-suited to security-conscious industries like finance and healthcare. Team plans start at $18/user/month.

mkt-phrasee.summary: AI copywriting that boosts email open rates
mkt-phrasee.description: Phrasee is an AI marketing language optimization platform that uses NLP to automatically generate email subject lines, push notifications, and SMS copy, then learns from real A/B test results to continuously improve open and click-through rates. It's used by major retailers and airlines like Walgreens and Virgin Atlantic, with reported average open-rate gains of over 10% per campaign. Pricing is custom enterprise-only.

write-rytr.summary: Best value for money, with solid Korean-language support
write-rytr.description: An AI writing tool that generates content for 40+ use cases—blogs, ads, emails, and more—in 30+ languages, at an affordable price of $9/mo (Saver) or $29/mo (Unlimited). Korean output quality is relatively stable, and a Chrome extension lets you use it anywhere. The free plan supports up to 10,000 characters of generation per month.
pros: Affordable at $9/mo, with stable Korean support
Supports 40+ content types and 30+ languages
Free plan generates up to 10,000 characters/month
cons: Doesn't match GPT-4-level quality
Quality drops for long-form writing (2,000+ characters)

write-writesonic.summary: Writes blog posts powered by up-to-date Google data
write-writesonic.description: An AI content platform that produces up-to-date SEO blog posts by integrating real-time Google search data. It includes Chatsonic (a GPT-4 + web-search chatbot) and AI Article Writer 5.0. It supports 60+ languages and 100+ content types, with built-in team collaboration features. Individual plans start at $16/mo.
pros: Writes with up-to-date info via real-time Google search
Supports 60 languages and 100+ content types
Includes the GPT-4-powered Chatsonic chatbot
cons: Starts at $16/mo, pricier than Rytr
Output quality can vary inconsistently between requests

write-sudowrite.summary: A creative-writing assistant for novelists (description and plot suggestions)
write-sudowrite.description: An AI creative-writing tool built specifically for fiction writers, offering 'Describe,' 'Story Engine,' and 'Feedback' features that break through writer's block and suggest what happens next. It's especially popular among genre fiction, fantasy, and romance writers, and a favorite of NaNoWriMo participants. Plans range from $10/mo (Hobby) to $100/mo (Max).
pros: Novel-writing-specific features (Describe, Story Engine)
Tailored for genre fiction, fantasy, and romance writers
Affordable $10/mo Hobby plan available
cons: Not well suited for marketing or business writing
Limited support for Korean-language fiction writing

write-novelai.summary: A subculture-focused AI that continues your story exactly the way you like it
write-novelai.description: A subculture-focused AI platform that generates text and images in the style of Japanese anime and light novels. Its strengths are an uncensored creative environment and a customizable writing module (Lorebook). Thanks to self-hosted servers, it also appeals to users who prioritize data privacy. Plans run from $10/mo (Tablet) to $25/mo (Opus).
pros: Uncensored creative environment for subculture content
Lorebook lets you set up detailed worldbuilding
Local data storage helps protect privacy
cons: Not well suited to genres outside anime and light novels
No free option; plans run $10-$25/mo

write-wordtune.summary: Turns flat sentences into vivid, polished writing
write-wordtune.description: A sentence-rewriting tool from AI21 Labs that instantly rephrases the same content in different tones and lengths—casual, formal, shorter, longer, and more. As a Chrome extension, it works right inside Gmail, Google Docs, and similar apps, making it especially useful for non-native English speakers writing emails and reports. Available with a free plan or Premium at $9.99/mo.
pros: Instantly rewrites sentences in various tones and lengths
Chrome extension works directly in Gmail and Google Docs
Especially useful for non-native English speakers writing emails
cons: English only, no Korean sentence-rewriting support
Specializes in sentence-level edits rather than full content generation

write-jenni.summary: Suggests sentences and manages citations for papers and essays
write-jenni.description: An AI writing assistant specialized in academic papers and essays that automatically suggests what to write next as you type and auto-inserts citations (APA/MLA/Chicago, etc.). Built-in plagiarism checking and source management make it popular with grad students and researchers. Available with a free plan (200 words/day) or a $12/mo plan.

write-type.summary: Predicts your next sentence as fast as you can type
write-type.description: An AI document editor that predicts and suggests your next sentence in real time as you type, continuing your writing in a natural flow that fits the context—no prompts needed. Its clean, minimal editor UI suits users who prefer a distraction-free writing environment. The $29/mo Pro plan is the main offering.

write-hemingway.summary: Revises overly complex sentences into clear, readable prose
write-hemingway.description: An editing tool that uses a Readability Grade score and color-coded highlights to instantly flag overly complex sentences, adverb overuse, and passive voice, helping you revise toward a clean, concise style. It was originally a rule-based tool with no AI, but Hemingway Plus has since added AI rewriting features. The web version is free; the desktop app is a one-time $19.99 purchase.

write-hypefury.description: An AI social media tool specialized in growing your presence on Twitter (X), offering automatic thread generation, optimal-time scheduling, and repurposing of top-performing posts. A standout growth-hacking feature is its 'Auto DM + Plug,' which automatically attaches a product-promo tweet whenever a follower retweets you. Plans range from $19/mo (Personal) to $49/mo (Business).

write-blaze.summary: Auto-completes frequently used phrases with keyboard shortcuts (AI included)
write-blaze.description: A text automation tool that instantly inserts frequently used email replies, report templates, and customer-service phrases via short keyboard shortcuts (snippets), letting whole teams share the same snippets to standardize communication. An added AI Assist feature automatically tailors snippet content to the situation. Offers a free plan and team plans starting at $6.99/user/mo.

edu-scispace.summary: An AI for researchers, from paper search to analysis and summarization
edu-scispace.description: A research platform that uses AI to search, summarize, and answer questions across a database of over 280 million academic papers. Upload a PDF and chat with it to ask questions about the paper and get difficult concepts explained in plain language. Offers a free plan and a Premium plan at $12/mo.
pros: AI search across a database of 280 million+ papers
Chat-based help understanding papers after uploading a PDF
Free plan available, Premium at $12/mo
cons: Limited coverage of Korean-language paper databases
Designed primarily around English-language papers

edu-elicit.summary: Ask a research question and it finds relevant papers and organizes the answers
edu-elicit.description: An AI research assistant that takes a research question in natural language, searches Semantic Scholar's 180-million-paper database for relevant studies, and organizes the key findings into a comparison table. It extracts selection criteria, methodology, and results by column, letting you put together a systematic literature review quickly. Free for 10 papers/month, then $12/mo on the Plus plan.
pros: Systematic literature reviews built on 180 million papers
Automatically organizes key findings into a comparison table
10 papers/month free, then an affordable $12/mo plan
cons: Focused on natural sciences and medicine, weaker in humanities
Mostly English-language papers, limited Korean research search

edu-consensus.summary: Answers questions that need scientific evidence, backed by academic papers
edu-consensus.description: An AI search engine that takes a scientific question like "Does coffee affect sleep?" and answers with a Yes/No/Unclear consensus meter backed by supporting studies drawn from 200 million peer-reviewed papers. It doesn't fabricate sources, making it highly trustworthy, and it provides GPT-4-based summaries. Offers a free plan and a Premium plan at $8.99/mo.
pros: Trustworthy, paper-backed answers with no fabricated sources
Clear conclusions via a Yes/No/Unclear consensus meter
Free plan available, affordable Premium at $8.99/mo
cons: Built for scientific questions, not suited to subjective ones
Can be slow to reflect the very latest papers

edu-khan.summary: A Socratic 1:1 learning tutor built by Khan Academy
edu-khan.description: An AI tutor built by Khan Academy in partnership with GPT-4 that doesn't hand you the answer outright, instead guiding students to discover concepts themselves through Socratic questioning. It covers math, science, and humanities across the board, and also includes lesson-planning support for teachers. A free program is available for students and teachers in the US.
pros: Encourages self-directed learning through Socratic questioning
Covers math, science, and humanities across the board
Free program offered through Khan Academy
cons: Currently US-focused, with limited Korean-language support
Doesn't give answers right away, which can be frustrating in a hurry

edu-quivr.summary: A second brain built by training AI on the files (knowledge) on your computer
edu-quivr.description: An open-source tool that lets you upload personal documents—PDFs, Notion pages, text files, and more—to build your own AI knowledge base. Since it can be self-hosted, users keep full data sovereignty, and you can choose from multiple AI models (GPT-4, Claude, etc.) to power it. Cloud plans start at $19/mo.

edu-humata.summary: Reads hundreds of pages of PDF in an instant and answers your questions
edu-humata.description: An AI document analysis tool that, once you upload a large PDF, analyzes the entire document, answers natural-language questions instantly, and points to the source page. It's useful for professionals who need to get up to speed fast on complex documents like legal contracts, technical manuals, and research reports. Offers a free plan (60 pages/month) and an Unlimited plan at $14.99/mo.

edu-scholarcy.summary: Distills complex papers down to the essentials in flashcard form
edu-scholarcy.description: An AI tool that, when you upload an academic paper, report, or article, automatically extracts the research purpose, methodology, conclusions, and key figures and summarizes them as flashcards.
