Summary cards can be exported to Notion, Zotero, and similar tools, and a browser extension lets you analyze papers on the spot while browsing online. Runs on a $9.99/mo Personal plan.

edu-gradescope.summary: Automates grading of exams in math, coding, and more with AI (for teachers)
edu-gradescope.description: An AI grading platform run by Turnitin that lets you scan handwritten exam papers or upload digital submissions, with AI grouping similar answers to enable batch grading. Used by 1,500+ institutions including MIT and Stanford, it's reported to improve grading consistency and cut instructors' grading time by an average of 90%. Available through institutional contracts.

edu-socratic.summary: A homework helper from Google—snap a photo to find the solution
edu-socratic.description: A learning-support app from Google that lets you photograph a homework problem with your camera, and AI instantly surfaces related concepts, solution methods, and YouTube videos. It supports a range of subjects including math, science, English, and history, and is designed to build conceptual understanding rather than just hand over answers. Free on iOS and Android.

edu-photomath.summary: Point your camera at a math problem and it walks you through the full solution
edu-photomath.description: An app that recognizes math problems through your smartphone camera and explains the step-by-step solution with animations—the largest math-learning tool in the world, with 220 million downloads. It supports everything from basic arithmetic to calculus and statistics, and offers concept explanations at each step of the solution. Free by default, with Photomath Plus at $59.99/year.
pros: World's largest math app, with 220 million downloads
Animated, step-by-step solution walkthroughs
Free core features covering everything from arithmetic to calculus
cons: Math-only, no support for other subjects
Can be used to just copy the solution without understanding the reasoning

chat-chatbase.summary: Feed in your own data and get a customer-support chatbot up in minutes
chat-chatbase.description: A no-code platform that trains a custom GPT-4-powered chatbot on your own data—PDFs, Notion pages, website URLs, and more—in as little as 5 minutes. You can embed it as a widget on your site or connect it via API, with built-in response history and conversation analytics. Plans start with a free tier (1 chatbot) and the $19/mo Hobby plan.
pros: Train a custom GPT-4 chatbot on your own data in 5 minutes
Start free right away, with Hobby plans from $19/mo
Supports both website widget and API deployment
cons: Free plan limited to 1 chatbot with a message cap
Not well suited for complex, multi-step automation

chat-botsonic.summary: An enterprise AI chatbot builder you can set up without writing code
chat-botsonic.description: A GPT-4-powered customer support chatbot builder from Writesonic that lets you train on your own data, customize the look to match your brand colors and logo, and deploy across your website, Slack, WhatsApp, and more. Built-in live conversation monitoring and escalation make it a good fit for everyone from small e-commerce shops to SaaS companies. Plans start at $49/mo with the Basic tier.
pros: Supports multi-channel deployment across Slack, WhatsApp, and more
Built-in live conversation monitoring and escalation
High-quality customer support powered by GPT-4
cons: Starts at $49/mo, pricier than Chatbase
Pricing can feel steep for small teams relative to the features offered

chat-dante.summary: A GPT-4-powered chatbot platform trained on your own custom data
chat-dante.description: A platform that trains GPT-4-powered chatbots on data pulled from files, websites, YouTube, Google Drive, and more, then lets you embed the trained bot on your site with a simple snippet of code. It also supports voice recognition and image analysis, and offers a white-label option so you can resell it under your own brand. Plans start at $19/mo with the Starter tier.

chat-myshell.summary: Build and share bots with distinct voices and personalities
chat-myshell.description: A platform for building multimodal AI bots—combining text, voice, and image—without writing any code, then sharing them with the community. You can browse and use other creators' bots in the marketplace. Real-time voice conversation and character AI are standout features, and creators earn revenue based on bot usage. Free to use, with a paid credit system for additional usage.

chat-poe.summary: Use multiple LLMs in one place and build your own bots
chat-poe.description: An AI model hub run by Quora that lets you compare and use a range of LLMs—ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, Mistral, and more—all in a single app. You can build custom bots, publish them to the community, and earn creator revenue based on usage. There's a free plan and a $19.99/mo Poe Pro subscription.
pros: Compare ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and more all in one place
Built-in ecosystem for creating and monetizing custom bots
Access to all the major AI models for $19.99/mo with Pro
cons: Relies entirely on third-party models rather than developing its own
Bot quality depends heavily on the skill of the community creator

chat-character.summary: The hottest playground for chatting with celebrities and anime characters
chat-character.description: An AI character chat platform with over 20 million monthly users, where you can freely chat with millions of bots—celebrities, anime characters, game characters, and more—or create and share your own. It's wildly popular among Gen Z and gets used for everything from language learning to roleplay to casual emotional conversation. Free to use, with c.ai+ available for $9.99/mo.
pros: The largest AI character platform among Gen Z, with 20 million monthly users
Millions of characters used for roleplay, language learning, and more
Free to use, with an affordable $9.99/mo c.ai+ upgrade
cons: Built for entertainment rather than business use
Chatbot quality falls short of GPT-4-level performance

chat-janitor.summary: An unfiltered character chatbot with no content restrictions
chat-janitor.description: An AI chatbot platform that allows NSFW character roleplay after age verification, with a community ecosystem built around user-created characters. Its flexible architecture lets you connect your own OpenAI or Claude API key to chat with the model of your choice. Free to use, with unlimited messages available through a Janitor Pro subscription.

chat-replika.summary: The longest-running AI friend, built to form a real emotional bond
chat-replika.description: An AI companion app launched in 2017 that learns your speech patterns and interests over time, growing into a personalized AI friend the more you talk with it. Studies have found it effective at reducing loneliness and anxiety, and it's used by over 10 million people. AR lets you interact with a 3D avatar in real physical space, and a Pro subscription ($29.99/mo) unlocks romantic relationship settings.
pros: A proven AI companion since 2017, with 10M+ users
An AI friend that grows more personalized the more you talk
Backed by research showing it reduces loneliness and anxiety
cons: Raises concerns about fostering emotional dependency; heed mental health experts' cautions
Romantic features require the $29.99/mo Pro subscription

chat-talkie.summary: A mobile hit for emotional chat and character collecting
chat-talkie.description: A mobile app for voice chatting and roleplaying with anime-style AI characters, growing quickly among teen and twenty-something users. Collecting and leveling up characters with distinct personalities adds a game-like layer that keeps people coming back. Free to use, with additional features available via in-app purchase.

chat-crushon.summary: An uncensored chat service built for romance and roleplay
chat-crushon.description: An adult AI romance and roleplay platform that allows NSFW content, offering thousands of pre-made characters alongside user-created ones. Image generation and voice chat add to the immersive virtual relationship experience. Plans include a free tier (20 messages/day) and the $4.90/mo Standard plan.

avatar-heygen.summary: Turn a single photo into a talking video with best-in-class lip sync
avatar-heygen.description: A platform that turns a single photo or video clip into a highly realistic AI avatar, then produces talking videos from nothing more than a text script—with lip-sync accuracy rated among the best in the industry. It supports dubbing in 40+ languages and real-time avatar streaming, and is widely used for marketing, e-commerce, and educational content. Plans start at $29/mo with the Creator tier.
pros: Industry-leading lip-sync accuracy
Supports dubbing in 40+ languages and real-time avatar streaming
Wide range of use cases across marketing, e-commerce, and education
cons: Starts at $29/mo for Creator; the free plan offers limited generation
High-quality avatars are only available on paid plans

avatar-did.summary: A veteran leader in talking-face generation APIs
avatar-did.description: Founded in 2017 as a deepfake-detection security startup, D-ID has since become a leading platform for generating talking avatar videos, with easy API integration into other apps as a key strength. Its 'Creative Reality Studio' lets anyone produce photorealistic presenter videos. Offers a free trial, with paid plans starting at $5.9/mo for the Lite tier.
pros: Founded in 2017, a pioneer in the talking-avatar space
Easy API integration lets you embed it into other apps effortlessly
Low barrier to entry with the $5.9/mo Lite plan
cons: Lip sync feels less natural than HeyGen's
Costs rise quickly once you need premium avatars

avatar-synthesia.summary: A professional-grade AI avatar built for corporate training and promo videos
avatar-synthesia.description: A professional platform offering 160+ AI avatars spanning different ethnicities, ages, and genders, with support for 120+ languages, optimized for global corporate e-learning and internal communications videos. Used by over 50,000 companies including Heineken, Zoom, and Nvidia, it can export directly to your LMS in SCORM or xAPI format. Plans start at $22/mo with the Starter tier.
pros: 160 avatars and 120 languages, purpose-built for global corporate training
Direct LMS export in SCORM and xAPI formats
A training video platform proven across 50,000+ companies
cons: Starting at $22/mo, it's pricey for individual users
Built specifically for corporate training, so it's overkill for entertainment content

avatar-akool.summary: High-quality face-swapping (deepfake) and avatar generation tool
avatar-akool.description: An AI visual marketing tool that handles real-time face swapping, avatar generation, background removal, and personalized video creation all in one platform. It's used to swap faces in existing videos or to mass-produce 1:1 personalized videos for email marketing campaigns. Plans start at $30/mo with the Starter tier.
pros: All-in-one toolkit for face swapping, avatars, and background removal
Mass-produce personalized videos for email marketing
Reasonable Starter pricing at $30/mo
cons: Raises ethical concerns around potential deepfake misuse
Advanced features come with a learning curve

avatar-hedra.summary: A next-generation avatar with fluid emotional expression and head movement
avatar-hedra.description: A next-generation avatar AI that takes an image and an audio clip and generates a talking video complete with natural emotional expression, blinking, and head movement. It feels far less stiff than earlier services, with richer expressiveness that produces results closer to live-action footage. Currently in free beta, with paid plans planned for the future.

avatar-virbo.summary: A budget-friendly avatar option for global commercial video production
avatar-virbo.description: An AI avatar video tool from Wondershare that offers 120+ multilingual avatars and realistic face-cloning at a relatively affordable price. Available on both mobile and desktop apps, it's especially handy for e-commerce product promos and social media marketing videos. There's a free plan and a $89.99/year paid plan.

avatar-argil.summary: Deepfake cloning and video generation built for influencers
avatar-argil.description: A platform that lets creators and influencers turn their own face and voice into an AI clone, so they can auto-generate large volumes of short-form content without appearing on camera themselves. Just 3 minutes of training footage is enough to produce a high-quality AI clone, and videos export directly in TikTok and Instagram Reels formats. Plans start at $49/mo with the Creator tier.

avatar-pipio.summary: Make videos from text alone, no actors required
avatar-pipio.description: A platform that produces professional-looking videos from a text script alone, drawing on a 'Digital Actor' library filmed with real actors. It lets you quickly create marketing videos with a range of people, backgrounds, and styles—with no filming costs—making it popular with ad agencies and e-commerce brands. Plans start at $29/mo with the Basic tier.

avatar-colossyan.summary: An avatar platform purpose-built for workplace learning & development videos
avatar-colossyan.description: An AI avatar platform built specifically for corporate learning & development (L&D) videos, offering 70+ diverse avatars and support for 70+ languages. Its standout feature—automatically converting an uploaded PowerPoint file into an AI-avatar training video—is especially popular with HR teams. Plans start at $28/mo with the Starter tier.
pros: Automatically converts uploaded PowerPoint files into AI-avatar training videos
70 avatars and 70 languages, purpose-built for HR training
Reasonable Starter pricing at $28/mo for corporate training
cons: Built for internal training, less suited to external marketing
Fewer avatars and languages than Synthesia

avatar-hourone.summary: High-definition virtual humans in a news-broadcast style
avatar-hourone.description: A professional video platform that produces high-definition AI avatars on par with news anchors and broadcast presenters, offering 100+ premium avatars and newsroom/studio background templates. It's well suited to enterprise customers who regularly mass-produce video newsletters, internal broadcasts, or external briefing videos. Plans start at $25/mo with the Lite tier.

web-framer.summary: A tool for designers to build high-quality websites without coding
web-framer.description: A no-code builder with a Figma-like visual editing interface for creating interactive websites, featuring an 'AI Site' function that auto-generates entire layouts and content from just a text description. It outputs React-based code and comes with built-in custom domains and analytics. Available with a free plan and a $10/mo Mini plan.
pros: No-code interactive websites with Figma-level UI
AI Site generates full layouts from a text description
Free plan available, affordable $10/mo Mini plan to start
cons: Being React-based, fine-grained customization requires dev knowledge
Limited for implementing complex backend functionality

web-v0.summary: Describe it in text and it writes React code instantly
web-v0.description: An AI UI generation tool from Vercel that instantly produces production-grade React code built on shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS from a text description of the component or page you want. You can preview the generated code and deploy straight to Vercel or paste it into a Next.js project. Offers free credits and a $20/mo Pro plan.
pros: Generates production-grade React code with shadcn/ui + Tailwind
One-click Vercel deployment and Next.js integration
Free credits included, big boost to developer productivity
cons: Hard to make the most of the output without basic React/Tailwind knowledge
Complex app logic still needs additional development

web-websim.summary: Simulates any website you can imagine
web-websim.description: A creative experimental tool that generates, on the fly, what a website would look like if you enter a fictional URL that doesn't actually exist. Clicking links inside a generated site spawns further imaginary linked pages, enabling an endless web simulation. Currently free to use.

web-create.summary: An attempt to automate the entire app development process
web-create.description: An AI app builder that automates designing, developing, and deploying a full-stack web app from just a text description, handling everything from UI generation to backend logic and database setup in one flow. Positioned to compete with Bolt.new and Lovable, it aims to let non-developers build production-level apps. Free during beta, with paid plans planned afterward.

web-relume.summary: Generates sitemaps/wireframes for Figma and Webflow
web-relume.description: An AI web design tool that auto-generates a full sitemap and wireframes from a description of your website's purpose and business, and exports them instantly to Figma or Webflow. With a library of 1,000+ components and AI copywriting features, it dramatically cuts planning and design time for web agencies and freelance designers. Offered at $38/mo.
pros: Auto-generates sitemaps and wireframes, exports to Figma
Library of 1,000+ components
Cuts planning and design time for agencies and freelancers
cons: $38/mo is pricey for individual designers
Real value only for Figma/Webflow users

web-mixo.summary: One line of an idea and a landing page appears instantly
web-mixo.description: An AI site builder that generates a polished landing page in 10 seconds from a single sentence describing your startup idea or product. It has a built-in email waitlist feature, making it popular for validating demand before an MVP launch. Available with a free plan and a $9/mo Starter plan.
pros: Turns a one-line idea into a landing page in 10 seconds
Built-in email waitlist for pre-launch demand validation
Low barrier to entry with a free plan and $9/mo Starter
cons: Landing-page specialized, can't build complex websites
Less design freedom than Framer

web-dora.summary: Build flashy 3D websites just by typing commands
web-dora.description: An AI builder for creating high-quality, no-code websites rich with 3D animation and interactive effects, letting you freely place Three.js-based 3D components via text commands or drag-and-drop. Well suited for sites where visual impact matters, like portfolios, product showcases, and startup landing pages. Offers a free plan and a $14/mo Starter plan.

web-10web.summary: AI automatically builds and manages your WordPress site
web-10web.description: A service where AI automatically builds a custom WordPress site from your business type and description, then deploys it on Google Cloud-based hosting. It also lets you enter a competitor's site URL to analyze and automatically recreate a similarly structured site. Hosting included starting at the $10/mo Personal plan.
pros: AI auto-builds a WordPress site with GCP hosting included
Analyzes competitor sites and recreates similar structures
Hosting bundled in even at the $10/mo Personal plan
cons: Being WordPress-based, still needs speed and security upkeep
Polish lags behind Framer or Mixo among AI builders

web-pineapple.summary: Quickly generates a blog or portfolio site
web-pineapple.description: An AI personal site builder for creators and freelancers that quickly generates a portfolio, blog, or Linktree-alternative site from a simple questionnaire. Its standout feature is that AI keeps the site automatically up to date as you keep adding content. Offered with a free plan and a $9/mo Pro plan.

web-galileo.summary: Generates mobile UI designs (Figma) from text
web-galileo.description: An AI design tool that generates mobile and web app UI designs from a text prompt and exports them straight to a Figma file. It automatically applies polished component hierarchies along with proper spacing and typography, greatly cutting the time designers spend on mockups. Currently accessible via waitlist.

game-rosebud.summary: A platform for making games by describing them in words, no coding needed
game-rosebud.description: A no-code game development platform where you describe a game idea in natural-language conversation and AI generates playable browser game code. You can play, edit, and deploy directly in the browser, and AI also generates the game assets (sprites, backgrounds). You can share games with the community and fork others' games to modify them. Free plan available.
pros: Describe it in natural language and get browser game code automatically
AI also generates assets and sprites
Free plan available, community forking and sharing supported
cons: Complex game logic still requires manual editing
Better suited to prototyping and learning than commercial games

game-scenario.summary: Train it on your game's art style for endless generation
game-scenario.description: An AI asset generation platform for game studios: fine-tune it on an existing game's art style to endlessly generate consistent characters, items, and backgrounds. Maintaining style consistency is its core strength, greatly boosting art team productivity in commercial game development. Also supports pipeline integration via API, offered starting at $19/mo Basic.
pros: Fine-tunes on your game's art style while keeping it consistent
Dramatically boosts commercial game art team productivity
API integration enables automated dev pipelines
cons: Starting at $19/mo Basic, with upfront cost to train the style
Weaker than Midjourney for non-game image generation

game-layer.summary: A pro-grade generation tool specialized for game asset production
game-layer.description: A platform offering an AI asset production workflow specialized for game developers and artists, capable of mass-generating textures, sprites, icons, and other game assets in high resolution. It supports team project collaboration and version control, and can be integrated into dev pipelines via API for automation. Offered with enterprise custom pricing.
pros: Mass-generates textures, sprites, icons, and other game assets
Supports team collaboration, version control, and API pipeline integration
High-resolution asset output ensures professional quality
cons: Enterprise custom pricing makes costs opaque
Less accessible for small indie developers

game-inworld.summary: Gives NPCs intelligence and personality so they can hold conversations
game-inworld.description: An AI character engine that gives game NPCs memory, emotion, goals, and personality so they can converse freely with players, easily integrated via SDK with Unity and Unreal Engine. Partners include Google, LG, and NetEase, and its AI characters understand the game's story context and react accordingly. Free to use, with custom pricing for commercial use.
pros: A free-conversation engine giving NPCs memory, emotion, and goals
Easy integration via Unity/Unreal SDK, Google is a partner
Free to use, with custom commercial pricing
cons: Real-time AI conversation can drive up server costs
NPC quality varies a lot depending on design

game-leonardo.summary: Powerful models built for generating game items and textures
game-leonardo.description: An AI image generation platform with numerous custom models specialized for game asset creation (3D Texture, Pixel Art, RPG 4.0, and more), letting you mass-produce characters, weapons, and environment art in a consistent style. It has also added motion image and 3D texture generation, extending its use across game production beyond just 2D images. Offers a free plan (150 tokens/day) and a $10/mo Apprentice plan.
pros: Game-specialized models including 3D Texture, Pixel Art, and RPG
Free plan with 150 tokens/day, low barrier to entry
Mass-generates characters, weapons, and environment art in consistent style
cons: Easily confused with the original Leonardo.ai, overkill for non-game uses
Falls behind Meshy for complex 3D model generation

game-skybox.summary: Generates 360-degree skybox images for game backgrounds
game-skybox.description: A specialized AI tool that generates 360-degree panoramic skybox images ready to use in games, VR, and AR environments from a text prompt. It outputs HDR-quality equirectangular images that can be imported straight into Unity or Unreal Engine, with style presets spanning fantasy, sci-fi, realistic, horror, and more. Offers a free plan and a $14/mo Pro plan.

game-meshy.summary: Quickly generates 3D game objects from text
game-meshy.description: A game-focused 3D AI tool that generates 3D models from text or 2D images within minutes, exportable in game-engine-ready formats like FBX, OBJ, and GLB. It also supports automatic texture generation and animation rigging, letting you quickly obtain game objects without a 3D artist. Offers a free plan and a $20/mo Pro plan.

game-promethean.summary: Automatically places and fills in game environments (maps)
game-promethean.description: An AI tool that automates scene composition and environment dressing in games: tell it something like "naturally fill the area around this forest cabin with trees and rocks" inside a 3D engine, and it automatically places the appropriate assets. It integrates into existing workflows via Unreal, Maya, and Max plugins, and claims to cut level designers' repetitive placement work by more than 70%. Free plan available.

game-charisma.summary: A character dialogue engine for interactive story games
game-charisma.description: A story-driven AI dialogue engine for interactive fiction, games, and educational simulations, letting you set detailed personality, memory, and goals for each character and build complex branching story trees with a visual node editor based on player choices. It can be integrated with Unity, Unreal, and WebGL, and has been used in real content by companies like BBC Studios. Offered starting at $15/mo Creator plan.

game-replica.summary: Solves game character voice acting with AI
game-replica.description: An AI voice acting platform for games, animation, and the metaverse, offering 100+ AI voice actors capable of diverse emotional expression and delivery. It highlights an ethical approach as its strength — partnering with real voice actors to use licensed AI voices — and can be integrated into game dev workflows via Unity and Unreal plugins. Offered starting at $24/mo Creator plan.

free-google-ai-studio.name: Google AI Studio
free-google-ai-studio.summary: Hundreds of free Gemini requests a day — the most generous free tier around
free-google-ai-studio.description: Google's free AI workbench lets you use the latest Gemini models straight from the browser with no payment set up. The daily request allowance is far more generous than other free services, so heavy jobs like long-document summarisation or code analysis are no problem. Best of all, you also get a free API key you can wire straight into your own projects.
free-google-ai-studio.strength: Hundreds of free requests a day, plus a free API key
pros: The most generous daily allowance of any free service
A free API key you can wire into your own projects
A huge context window handles long documents in one pass
cons: On the free tier your input may be used to improve the models
The developer-oriented interface takes some getting used to
free-google-ai-studio.userReview: Giving away an API key for free is almost unfair. Start here if you want free AI.

free-chatgpt.name: ChatGPT (free plan)
free-chatgpt.summary: A set number of latest-model messages a day, then a lighter model keeps going
free-chatgpt.description: Sign up and you get a set number of messages a day on the latest model; once that runs out it switches automatically to a lighter model so the conversation never stops. Even the free plan includes image recognition, file uploads and web search, which covers most everyday work. With the largest user base of any AI, there is also far more guidance and prompt material to draw on.
free-chatgpt.strength: Image recognition, file uploads and web search, all on the free plan
pros: The conversation continues on a lighter model after the limit
File uploads and web search included on the free plan
The largest pool of guides and prompt examples anywhere
cons: The daily allowance of latest-model messages is tight
Responses slow down during busy periods
free-chatgpt.userReview: The free plan is genuinely usable. I like that the chat doesn't cut off when I hit the limit.

free-claude.name: Claude (free plan)
free-claude.summary: Best-in-class writing and document analysis, with the limit topping up every few hours
free-claude.description: Claude is particularly strong at understanding long documents and writing naturally, and the free plan includes a set amount of the latest model. The allowance refills every few hours rather than once a day, which suits spreading your usage across the day. It scores especially well on work that has to be both long and accurate — report drafts, contract review, paper summaries.
free-claude.strength: Long-document comprehension and writing quality, with a limit that refills
pros: The latest model is available even on the free plan
The limit refills every few hours, so you can spread usage out
High accuracy summarising and reviewing long documents
cons: The allowance goes quickly if you use it all at once
No image generation
free-claude.userReview: Best place for a report draft. The writing actually reads like a person wrote it.

free-perplexity.name: Perplexity (free plan)
free-perplexity.summary: AI search with sources attached — basic search is effectively unlimited for free
free-perplexity.description: Ask a question and Perplexity searches the live web, attaching a source link to every part of its answer. Basic search is effectively unlimited on the free plan; only the deeper advanced searches are capped at a few per day. Being able to check the evidence behind an answer makes it especially trustworthy for research and fact-checking.
free-perplexity.strength: A source link on every answer, with basic search effectively unlimited
pros: Sources attached to every answer make fact-checking easy
Basic search is virtually unlimited on the free plan
Very fast to reflect the latest information
cons: Advanced search is limited to a few uses a day
Not suited to long creative writing
free-perplexity.userReview: I search here instead of Google now. With sources attached I can cite it straight away.

free-deepseek.name: DeepSeek
free-deepseek.summary: A high-end reasoning model for free with effectively no cap — strong at maths and coding
free-deepseek.description: DeepSeek's high-performance AI is open on the web with no separate usage cap. It approaches paid-model performance on maths problems and code while costing nothing, which has made it popular for study and development. The model weights are open source, so you can also run it on your own server.
free-deepseek.strength: Effectively unlimited free use, with strong maths and coding reasoning
pros: Web chat has effectively no usage cap
Maths and coding reasoning on a par with paid models
Open source, so you can self-host it
cons: Data is stored on servers in China, so it is unsuitable for sensitive material
Answers are constrained on some political and historical topics
free-deepseek.userReview: I use it for coding problems. Surprised something free performs this well.

free-copilot.name: Microsoft Copilot
free-copilot.summary: A GPT-based chatbot plus image generation, with daily free image credits
free-copilot.description: Microsoft's free AI assistant is built into Windows and Edge, offering conversational answers and image generation together. Image generation comes with a daily allowance of fast credits; once they run out you can keep generating, just more slowly. Being available straight from the Windows taskbar and inside Office documents makes it the easiest to reach.
free-copilot.strength: Chat and image generation combined, built into Windows
pros: Daily free image credits included
You can keep generating after credits run out, just more slowly
Built into Windows, Edge and Office
cons: Answer length and creative freedom are limited
Promotional links sometimes appear in answers
free-copilot.userReview: It's already on Windows so I use it most. Free images are a nice bonus.

free-grok.name: Grok (free plan)
free-grok.summary: Strong on real-time information from X, with the free allowance resetting every few hours
free-grok.description: xAI's model references live posts on X (formerly Twitter) directly, making it strong on what is happening right now. The free plan locks after a set number of messages and reopens a few hours later, which suits spreading usage across the day. Image generation is included in the free allowance too.
free-grok.strength: Reflects real-time information from X, with image generation included free
pros: Answers reflect X posts from this very moment
The free allowance resets every few hours
Image generation included in the free tier
cons: The free allowance is small for a single sitting
Sources are social posts, so answers need fact-checking
free-grok.userReview: For breaking news it's noticeably faster than the other AIs.

free-leonardo.name: Leonardo.Ai
free-leonardo.summary: The benchmark for free image generation — 150 tokens topped up every day
free-leonardo.description: Leonardo tops your account up with tokens automatically every day, so you can create dozens of images without paying. Style-specific models and character-consistency tools make it especially popular for game assets and illustration work. Commercial use of what you create is permitted even on the free plan.
free-leonardo.strength: Tokens that top up daily, with commercial use permitted
pros: Tokens top up daily, so you can keep creating
Commercial use permitted on the free plan
Character-consistency tools included
cons: High-resolution and advanced models burn through tokens fast
Free-plan results appear in the public gallery
free-leonardo.userReview: Tokens refill every day, so there's no pressure — I just keep experimenting.

free-suno.name: Suno
free-suno.summary: Lyrics in, finished song out — daily free credits for a few tracks a day
free-suno.description: Enter lyrics and a genre and Suno produces a finished track with vocals. The free plan tops up credits daily, so you can steadily make a few songs a day. Songs made on the free plan are for non-commercial use only, so you will need to upgrade to use them in monetised content.
free-suno.strength: Daily credits that produce full songs with vocals
pros: Produces a finished song with vocals from lyrics alone
Credits top up every day
Genre and mood are easy to specify
cons: Free-plan songs cannot be used commercially
Longer tracks consume a lot of credits
free-suno.userReview: I use it for background music on shorts. A few songs a day is plenty.

free-canva-ai.name: Canva Magic Studio
free-canva-ai.summary: AI built into a design tool, with magic-tool usage included on free accounts
free-canva-ai.description: Generate images from text, remove backgrounds and auto-write copy from inside a design tool you already know. Free accounts get a periodic allowance of magic-tool usage, which is plenty for card news or thumbnails. Everything runs in the browser with nothing to install, so it has the lowest barrier to entry for non-specialists.
free-canva-ai.strength: Design and AI in one place, usable by non-specialists
pros: Design work and AI generation happen on the same screen
Magic-tool usage included on free accounts
A large template library keeps results looking polished
cons: Most advanced AI features need a paid upgrade
The free allowance resets monthly rather than daily
free-canva-ai.userReview: Fastest way to make a thumbnail. Background removal alone earns its keep.

free-colab.name: Google Colab
free-colab.summary: A few hours of free GPU a day — essential if you want to run AI yourself
free-colab.description: Google's free development environment runs Python in the browser and allocates you a GPU at no cost. There is a daily cap on GPU hours and long idle periods end your session, but it is more than enough to run open-source AI models yourself. Nothing to install makes it the most recommended starting point for learning AI.
free-colab.strength: Free GPU with nothing to install, for running open-source models
pros: A GPU allocated free so you can actually run models
Nothing to install — start straight from the browser
Plenty of tutorials and example notebooks
cons: Daily GPU hours are capped
Leave it idle too long and the session drops, losing your work
free-colab.userReview: I learned AI entirely on this. Never thought I'd be so grateful for a free GPU.

free-huggingface.name: Hugging Face Spaces
free-huggingface.summary: Try tens of thousands of other people's AI demos free, mostly without signing in
free-huggingface.description: Spaces hosts tens of thousands of AI demos uploaded by developers worldwide, all runnable straight from the browser. Most tools — image generation, voice conversion, background removal — can be tried for free without even signing in. Popular demos queue up, but it is the best place to compare several AIs before paying for any of them.
free-huggingface.strength: Tens of thousands of AI demos to try free, no sign-in needed
pros: Most demos run without signing in
The best place to compare AIs before paying
New open-source models appear here first
cons: Popular demos can have long queues
Demos vanish without warning if the creator takes them down
free-huggingface.userReview: I always try things here before spending money. There's an AI for everything.
